text.Rotherham: So Joram the king returned to get healed in Jezreel, from the wounds wherewith the Syrians had wounded him in Ramah, when he fought with Hazael king of Syria,—and, Ahaziah son of Jehoram king of Judah, went down to see Joram son of Ahab in Jezreel, because he, was sick.
text.KJV: 8:29 And king Ioram went backe to be healed in Iezreel, of the woundes which the Syrians had giuen him at Ramah, when hee fought against Hazael king of Syria: And Ahaziah the son of Iehoram king of Iudah, went downe to see Ioram the sonne of Ahab in Iezreel, because he was sicke.
text.NABRE: 8:29 King Joram returned to Jezreel to be healed of the wounds which the Arameans had inflicted on him at Ramah in his battle against Hazael, king of Aram. Then Ahaziah, son of Joram, king of Judah, went down to Jezreel to visit Joram, son of Ahab, for he was sick.

text.NRSV: Then the prophet Elisha called a member of the company of prophets and said to him, “Gird up your loins; take this flask of oil in your hand, and go to Ramoth-gilead.”
text.Rotherham: Now, Elisha the prophet, called one of the sons of the prophets,—and said to him—Gird up thy loins, and take this flask of oil in thy hand, and go to Ramoth-gilead;
text.KJV: 9:1 And Elisha the Prophet called one of the children of the Prophets, and said vnto him, Gird vp thy loines, and take this boxe of oile in thine hand, and goe to Ramoth Gilead.
text.NABRE: 9:1 Chapter 9 - Elisha and Jehu of Israel. Elisha the prophet called one of the guild prophets and said to him: “Get ready for a journey. Take this flask of oil with you, and go to Ramoth-gilead.

text.NRSV: When you arrive, look there for Jehu son of Jehoshaphat son of Nimshi; go in and get him to leave his companions, and take him into an inner chamber.
text.Rotherham: and, when thou art come in thither, then look thee out, there, Jehu son of Jehoshaphat, son of Nimshi; and thou shalt enter and get him to rise up out of the midst of his brethren, and take him into an inner chamber;
text.KJV: 9:2 And when thou commest thither, looke out there Iehu the sonne of Iehoshaphat, the sonne of Nimshi, and goe in, and make him arise vp from among his brethren, and carie him to an inner chamber.
text.NABRE: 9:2 When you get there, look for Jehu, son of Jehoshaphat, son of Nimshi. Enter and take him away from his companions and bring him into an inner chamber.

text.NRSV: Then take the flask of oil, pour it on his head, and say, ‘Thus says the Lord: I anoint you king over Israel.’ Then open the door and flee; do not linger.”
text.Rotherham: then shalt thou take the flask of oil, and pour out upon his head, and say—Thus, saith Yahweh, I have anointed thee to be king unto Israel! Then shalt thou open the door and flee, and not tarry.
text.KJV: 9:3 Then take the boxe of oile, and powre it on his head, and say, Thus saith the Lord, I haue anointed thee king ouer Israel: then open the doore, and flee, and tary not.
text.NABRE: 9:3 From the flask you have, pour oil on his head, and say, ‘Thus says the LORD: I anoint you king over Israel.’ Then open the door and flee without delay.”

text.NRSV: So the young man, the young prophet, went to Ramoth-gilead.
text.Rotherham: So the young man went his way—the young man the prophet—unto Ramoth-gilead.
text.KJV: 9:4 ¶ So the yong man, euen the yong man the Prophet, went to Ramoth Gilead:
text.NABRE: 9:4 The aide (the prophet’s aide) went to Ramoth-gilead.

text.NRSV: He arrived while the officers of the army were in council, and he said, “I have a message for you, commander.” Jehu asked, “For which one of us?” He answered, “For you, commander.”
text.Rotherham: And, when he came up, lo! the captains of the force, sitting, and he said—A word, have I, for thee, O captain! And Jehu said—For which, of us all? And he said,—For thee, O captain!
text.KJV: 9:5 And when hee came, behold, the captaines of the host were sitting; and hee said, I haue an errand to thee, O captaine: And Iehu said, Unto which of all vs? And he said, To thee, O captaine.
text.NABRE: 9:5 When he arrived, the commanders of the army were in session. He said, “I have a message for you, commander.” Jehu asked, “For which one of us?” “For you, commander,” he answered.

text.NRSV: So Jehu got up and went inside; the young man poured the oil on his head, saying to him, “Thus says the Lord the God of Israel: I anoint you king over the people of the Lord, over Israel.
text.Rotherham: Then rose he up and went inside, and poured out the oil upon his head, and said unto him—Thus, saith Yahweh, God of Israel, I have anointed thee to be king unto the people of Yahweh, unto Israel;
text.KJV: 9:6 And hee arose, and went into the house, and hee powred the oyle on his head, and said vnto him, Thus sayth the Lord God of Israel, I haue anoynted thee king ouer the people of the Lord, euen ouer Israel.
text.NABRE: 9:6 Jehu got up and went into the house. Then the prophet’s aide poured the oil on his head and said, “Thus says the LORD, the God of Israel: I anoint you king over the people of the LORD, over Israel.

text.NRSV: You shall strike down the house of your master Ahab, so that I may avenge on Jezebel the blood of my servants the prophets, and the blood of all the servants of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt smite the house of Ahab thy lord,—so will I avenge the blood of my servants the prophets, and the blood of all the servants of Yahweh, at the hand of Jezebel;
text.KJV: 9:7 And thou shalt smite the house of Ahab thy master, that I may auenge the blood of my seruants the Prophets, and the blood of all the seruants of the Lord, at the hand of Iezebel.
text.NABRE: 9:7 You shall destroy the house of Ahab your master; thus will I avenge the blood of my servants the prophets, and the blood of all the other servants of the LORD shed by Jezebel.

text.NRSV: For the whole house of Ahab shall perish; I will cut off from Ahab every male, bond or free, in Israel.
text.Rotherham: so shall perish all the house of Ahab,—and I will cut off to Ahab the meanest, both him that is shut up and him that is left at large, in Israel.
text.KJV: 9:8 For the whole house of Ahab shal perish, and I will cut off from Ahab, him that pisseth against the wall, and him that is shut vp and left in Israel.
text.NABRE: 9:8 The whole house of Ahab shall perish: I will cut off from Ahab’s line every male, whether bond or free in Israel.

text.NRSV: I will make the house of Ahab like the house of Jeroboam son of Nebat, and like the house of Baasha son of Ahijah.
text.Rotherham: Yea I will deliver up the house of Ahab, like the house of Jeroboam son of Nebat,—and like the house of Baasha son of Ahijah:
text.KJV: 9:9 And I will make the house of Ahab, like the house of Ieroboam the sonne of Nebat, and like the house of Baasha the sonne of Ahiiah.
text.NABRE: 9:9 I will make the house of Ahab like that of Jeroboam, son of Nebat, and like that of Baasha, son of Ahijah.

text.NRSV: The dogs shall eat Jezebel in the territory of Jezreel, and no one shall bury her.” Then he opened the door and fled.
text.Rotherham: And, as for Jezebel, the dogs shall eat [her], in the town-land of Jezreel, there being none to bury her. And he opened the door, and fled.
text.KJV: 9:10 And the dogges shal eate Iezebel in the portion of Iezreel, and there shal be none to burie her. And he opened the doore, and fled.
text.NABRE: 9:10 In the confines of Jezreel, the dogs shall devour Jezebel so that no one can bury her.” Then he opened the door and fled.

text.NRSV: When Jehu came back to his master’s officers, they said to him, “Is everything all right? Why did that madman come to you?” He answered them, “You know the sort and how they babble.”
text.Rotherham: Then, Jehu, came forth unto the servants of his lord, and one said to him—Is all well? why came this madman unto thee? And he said unto them, Ye, know, the man and his message.
text.KJV: 9:11 ¶ Then Iehu came foorth to the seruants of his lord, and one said vnto him, Is all well? wherefore came this madde fellow to thee? And he said vnto them, Yee know the man, and his communication.
text.NABRE: 9:11 When Jehu rejoined his master’s servants, they asked him, “Is all well? Why did that madman come to you?” He replied, “You know that kind of man and his talk.”

text.NRSV: They said, “Liar! Come on, tell us!” So he said, “This is just what he said to me: ‘Thus says the Lord, I anoint you king over Israel.’”
text.Rotherham: And they said—False! pray tell, us. And he said,—Thus and thus, spake he unto me, saying, Thus, saith Yahweh, I have anointed thee to be king unto Israel.
text.KJV: 9:12 And they said, It is false, tell vs now: And hee sayde, Thus and thus spake he to me, saying, Thus saith the Lord, I haue anoynted thee King ouer Israel.
text.NABRE: 9:12 But they said, “Tell us another lie!” So he told them, “This is what the prophet’s aide said to me, ‘Thus says the LORD: I anoint you king over Israel.’”

text.NRSV: Then hurriedly they all took their cloaks and spread them for him on the bare steps; and they blew the trumpet, and proclaimed, “Jehu is king.”
text.Rotherham: Then hasted they, and took, every man his garment, and put it under him, upon the very steps,—and blew with a horn, and said, Jehu, is king!
text.KJV: 9:13 Then they hasted, and tooke euery man his garment, and put it vnder him on the top of the staires, and blewe with trumpets, saying, Iehu is king.
text.NABRE: 9:13 At once each took his garment, spread it under Jehu on the bare steps, blew the horn, and cried out, “Jehu is king!”

text.NRSV: Thus Jehu son of Jehoshaphat son of Nimshi conspired against Joram. Joram with all Israel had been on guard at Ramoth-gilead against King Hazael of Aram;
text.Rotherham: Thus did Jehu son of Jehoshaphat son of Nimshi conspire against Joram,—when, Joram, was watching Ramoth-gilead, he and all Israel, because of Hazael king of Syria.
text.KJV: 9:14 So Iehu the sonne of Iehoshaphat, the sonne of Nimshi, conspired against Ioram: (now Ioram had kept Ramoth Gilead, hee, and all Israel, because of Hazael king of Syria:
text.NABRE: 9:14 VIII. The End of the Omrid Dynasty - Death of Joram of Israel. Jehu, son of Jehoshaphat, son of Nimshi, formed a conspiracy against Joram. (Joram, with all Israel, had been besieging Ramoth-gilead against Hazael, king of Aram,

text.NRSV: but King Joram had returned to Jezreel to be healed of the wounds that the Arameans had inflicted on him when he fought against King Hazael of Aram. So Jehu said, “If this is your wish, then let no one slip out of the city to go and tell the news in Jezreel.”
text.Rotherham: But Jehoram the king had returned to get himself healed in Jezreel, of the wounds wherewith the Syrians had wounded him, when he fought with Hazael king of Syria. Then said Jehu—If such is your mind, let no fugitive get forth out of the city, to go and tell it in Jezreel.
text.KJV: 9:15 But king Ioram was returned to bee healed in Iezreel, of the wounds which the Syrians had giuen him, when he fought with Hazael king of Syria.) And Iehu said, If it be your minds, then let none goe forth nor escape out of the citie, to goe to tell it in Iezreel.
text.NABRE: 9:15 but had returned to Jezreel to be healed of the wounds the Arameans had inflicted on him in the battle against Hazael, king of Aram.) Jehu said to them, “If this is what you really want, see that no one escapes from the city to report in Jezreel.”

text.NRSV: Then Jehu mounted his chariot and went to Jezreel, where Joram was lying ill. King Ahaziah of Judah had come down to visit Joram.
text.Rotherham: So Jehu rode in a chariot, and went towards Jezreel, for, Joram, was lying there,—and, Ahaziah king of Judah, had come down to see Joram.
text.KJV: 9:16 So Iehu rode in a charet, and went to Iezreel, (for Ioram lay there:) and Ahaziah king of Iudah was come downe to see Ioram.
text.NABRE: 9:16 Then Jehu mounted his chariot and drove to Jezreel, where Joram lay ill and Ahaziah, king of Judah, had come to visit him.

text.NRSV: In Jezreel, the sentinel standing on the tower spied Jehu’s company coming, and said, “I see a company.” Joram said, “Take a horseman and send him to meet them, and let him say, ‘Is it peace?’”
text.Rotherham: Now, the watchman, was standing upon the tower, in Jezreel, so he saw the great company of Jehu, when he came, and said—A great company, can I see! Then said Jehoram—Take a horseman and send to meet them, that he may say—Is it peace?
text.KJV: 9:17 And there stood a watchman on the towre in Iezreel, and hee spied the company of Iehu as he came, and said, I see a companie. And Ioram sayd, Take an horseman, and send to meete them, and let him say, Is it peace?
text.NABRE: 9:17 The watchman standing on the tower in Jezreel saw the troop of Jehu coming and reported, “I see chariots.” Joram said, “Get a driver and send him to meet them and to ask whether all is well.”

text.NRSV: So the horseman went to meet him and said, “Thus says the king, ‘Is it peace?’” Jehu responded, “What have you to do with peace? Fall in behind me.” The sentinel reported, saying, “The messenger reached them, but he is not coming back.”
text.Rotherham: So the horseman went to meet him, and said—Thus, saith the king, Is it peace? And Jehu said—What hast thou to do with peace? turn thee behind me. And the watchman told, saying, The messenger came up to them, but hath not turned back.
text.KJV: 9:18 So there went one on horsebacke to meete him, and said, Thus sayth the king, Is it peace? And Iehu said, What hast thou to doe with peace? turne thee behinde me. And the watchman tolde, saying, The messenger came to them, but he commeth not againe.
text.NABRE: 9:18 So a horseman went out to meet him and said, “The king asks, ‘Is everything all right?’” Jehu said, “What does it matter to you how things are? Get behind me.” The watchman reported to the king, “The messenger has reached them, but is not returning.”

text.NRSV: Then he sent out a second horseman, who came to them and said, “Thus says the king, ‘Is it peace?’” Jehu answered, “What have you to do with peace? Fall in behind me.”
text.Rotherham: Then sent he a second horseman, and he came up to them and said, Thus, saith the king, Is it peace? And Jehu said—What hast thou to do with peace? turn thee behind me.
text.KJV: 9:19 Then he sent out a second on horsebacke, which came to them, and sayd, Thus sayth the king, Is it peace? And Iehu answered, What hast thou to doe with peace? turne thee behinde me.
text.NABRE: 9:19 Joram sent a second horseman, who went to them and said, “The king asks, ‘Is everything all right?’” “What does it matter to you how things are?” Jehu replied. “Get behind me.”

text.NRSV: Again the sentinel reported, “He reached them, but he is not coming back. It looks like the driving of Jehu son of Nimshi; for he drives like a maniac.”
text.Rotherham: And the watchman told, saying, He came up to them, but hath not turned back,—and, the driving, is like the driving of Jehu son of Nimshi; for, with mad haste, doth he drive.
text.KJV: 9:20 And the watchman tolde, saying, He came euen vnto them, and commeth not againe: and the driuing is like the driuing of Iehu the sonne of Nimshi; for he driueth furiously.
text.NABRE: 9:20 The watchman reported, “He has reached them, but is not returning. The driving is like that of Jehu, son of Nimshi; he drives like a madman.”

text.NRSV: Joram said, “Get ready.” And they made his chariot ready. Then King Joram of Israel and King Ahaziah of Judah set out, each in his chariot, and went to meet Jehu; they met him at the property of Naboth the Jezreelite.
text.Rotherham: Then said Jehoram, Harness! So one harnessed his chariot,—and Jehoram king of Israel and Ahaziah king of Judah went forth, each man in his chariot, yea they went forth to meet Jehu, and came upon him in the heritage of Naboth the Jezreelite.
text.KJV: 9:21 And Ioram said, Make readie. And his charet was made ready. And Ioram king of Israel, and Ahaziah king of Iudah, went out, each in his charet, and they went out against Iehu, and met him in the portion of Naboth the Iezreelite.
text.NABRE: 9:21 “Hitch up my chariot,” said Joram, and they hitched up his chariot. Then Joram, king of Israel, and Ahaziah, king of Judah, set out, each in his own chariot, to meet Jehu. They reached him near the plot of ground of Naboth the Jezreelite.

text.NRSV: When Joram saw Jehu, he said, “Is it peace, Jehu?” He answered, “What peace can there be, so long as the many whoredoms and sorceries of your mother Jezebel continue?”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when Jehoram saw Jehu, that he said—Is it peace, Jehu? And he said—What can be the peace, while thy mother Jezebel’s harlotries and her incantations do so abound?
text.KJV: 9:22 And it came to passe when Ioram saw Iehu, that hee said, Is it peace, Iehu? And he answered, What peace, so long as the whoredomes of thy mother Iezebel, and her witchcrafts are so many?
text.NABRE: 9:22 When Joram recognized Jehu, he asked, “Is everything all right, Jehu?” Jehu replied, “How could everything be all right as long as all the harlotry and sorcery of your mother Jezebel continues?”

text.NRSV: Then Joram reined about and fled, saying to Ahaziah, “Treason, Ahaziah!”
text.Rotherham: Then Jehoram turned his hands, and fled,—and said unto Ahaziah—Treachery! O Ahaziah!
text.KJV: 9:23 And Ioram turned his hand, and fled, and said to Ahaziah, There is treachery, O Ahaziah.
text.NABRE: 9:23 Joram reined about and fled, crying to Ahaziah, “Treason, Ahaziah!”

text.NRSV: Jehu drew his bow with all his strength, and shot Joram between the shoulders, so that the arrow pierced his heart; and he sank in his chariot.
text.Rotherham: And, Jehu, bent his bow, and smote Jehoram, between his arms,—and the arrow came out at his heart,—and he sank down in his chariot.
text.KJV: 9:24 And Iehu drew a bowe with his full strength, and smote Iehoram betweene his armes, and the arrow went out at his heart, and hee sunke downe in his charet.
text.NABRE: 9:24 But Jehu had drawn his bow and he shot Joram between the shoulders, so that the arrow went through his heart and he collapsed in his chariot.

text.NRSV: Jehu said to his aide Bidkar, “Lift him up and throw him on the plot of ground belonging to Naboth the Jezreelite; for remember, when you and I rode side by side behind his father Ahab how the Lord uttered this oracle against him:
text.Rotherham: Then said he unto Bidkar his officer, Take [him] up, [and] cast him forth, into the field-portion of Naboth the Jezreelite,—for remember, when I and thou were riding as a couple together after Ahab his father, that, Yahweh, laid upon him this doom:
text.KJV: 9:25 Then said Iehu to Bidkar his captaine, Take vp, and cast him in the portion of the field of Naboth the Iezreelite: for remember, how that when I and thou rode together after Ahab his father, the Lord laide this burden vpon him:
text.NABRE: 9:25 Then Jehu said to his adjutant Bidkar, “Take him and throw him into the plot of ground in the field of Naboth the Jezreelite. For remember when you and I were driving teams behind Ahab his father, the LORD delivered this oracle against him:

text.NRSV: ‘For the blood of Naboth and for the blood of his children that I saw yesterday, says the Lord, I will repay you on this very plot of ground.’ Now therefore lift him up and throw him on the plot of ground, in accordance with the word of the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: Surely, the blood of Naboth and the blood of his sons, have I lately seen, declareth Yahweh, therefore will I requite thee in this portion, declareth Yahweh. Now, therefore, take [him] up, [and] cast him forth into the portion, according to the word of Yahweh.
text.KJV: 9:26 Surely I haue seene yesterday the blood of Naboth, and the blood of his sonnes, sayd the Lord, and I will requite thee in this plat, sayth the Lord. Now therefore take and cast him into the plat of ground, according to the word of the Lord.
text.NABRE: 9:26 As surely as I saw yesterday the blood of Naboth and the blood of his sons—oracle of the LORD—I will repay you for it in that very plot of ground—oracle of the LORD. So now take him and throw him into this plot of ground, in keeping with the word of the LORD.”

text.NRSV: When King Ahaziah of Judah saw this, he fled in the direction of Beth-haggan. Jehu pursued him, saying, “Shoot him also!” And they shot him in his chariot at the ascent to Gur, which is by Ibleam. Then he fled to Megiddo, and died there.
text.Rotherham: And, Ahaziah king of Judah, saw it, and fled by the way of the garden house,—and Jehu pursued him, and said—Him also! smite him in the chariot! It was in the ascent of Gur, which is by Ibleam. And he fled to Mepidgo, and died there.
text.KJV: 9:27 ¶ But when Ahaziah the king of Iudah saw this, hee fled by the way of the garden house: and Iehu followed after him, and said, Smite him also in the charet; and they did so, at the going vp to Gur, which is by Ibleam: And hee fled to Megiddo, and died there.
text.NABRE: 9:27 Death of Ahaziah of Judah. Seeing what was happening, Ahaziah, king of Judah, fled toward Beth-haggan. Jehu pursued him, shouting, “Him too!” They struck him as he rode through the pass of Gur near Ibleam, but he continued his flight as far as Megiddo and died there.

text.NRSV: His servants carried him in a chariot to Jerusalem, and buried him in his tomb with his ancestors in the city of David.
text.Rotherham: And his servants conveyed him in a chariot and brought him into Jerusalem,—and buried him in his sepulchre, with his fathers, in the city of David.
text.KJV: 9:28 And his seruants caried him in a charet to Ierusalem, and buried him in his sepulchre with his fathers, in the citie of Dauid.
text.NABRE: 9:28 His servants brought him in a chariot to Jerusalem and they buried him in his grave with his ancestors in the City of David.

text.NRSV: In the eleventh year of Joram son of Ahab, Ahaziah began to reign over Judah.
text.Rotherham: Now it was, in the eleventh year of Joram son of Ahab, that Ahaziah began to reign over Judah.
text.KJV: 9:29 And in the eleuenth yeere of Ioram the sonne of Ahab, began Ahaziah to reigne ouer Iudah.
text.NABRE: 9:29 In the eleventh year of Joram, son of Ahab, Ahaziah became king over Judah.

text.NRSV: When Jehu came to Jezreel, Jezebel heard of it; she painted her eyes, and adorned her head, and looked out of the window.
text.Rotherham: Now, when Jehu entered Jezreel and, Jezebel, heard of it, she set her eyes in stibium, and ornamented her head, and looked forth through the lattice.
text.KJV: 9:30 ¶ And when Iehu was come to Iezreel, Iezebel heard of it, and shee painted her face, and tyred her head, and looked out at a window.
text.NABRE: 9:30 Death of Jezebel. Jehu came to Jezreel, and when Jezebel heard of it, she shadowed her eyes, adorned her hair, and looked down from her window.

text.NRSV: As Jehu entered the gate, she said, “Is it peace, Zimri, murderer of your master?”
text.Rotherham: So, when, Jehu, had entered in at the gate, she said, Was it peace, when Zimri slew his lord?
text.KJV: 9:31 And as Iehu entred in at the gate, she said, Had Zimri peace, who slew his master?
text.NABRE: 9:31 As Jehu came through the gate, she cried out, “Is all well, you Zimri, murderer of your master?”

text.NRSV: He looked up to the window and said, “Who is on my side? Who?” Two or three eunuchs looked out at him.
text.Rotherham: And he lifted up his face unto the lattice, and said—Who, is with me? Who? And there looked out unto him, two or three eunuchs.
text.KJV: 9:32 And he lift vp his face to the window, and said, Who is on my side, who? And there looked out to him two or three Eunuches.
text.NABRE: 9:32 Jehu looked up to the window and shouted, “Who is on my side? Who?” At this, two or three eunuchs looked down toward him.

text.NRSV: He said, “Throw her down.” So they threw her down; some of her blood spattered on the wall and on the horses, which trampled on her.
text.Rotherham: And he said—Hurl her down. And they hurled her down,—and there was sprinkled of her blood—upon the wall, and upon the horses, and they trode upon her.
text.KJV: 9:33 And he said, Throw her downe. So they threw her downe, and some of her blood was sprinkled on the wall, and on the horses: and he trode her vnder foote.
text.NABRE: 9:33 “Throw her down,” he ordered. They threw her down, and some of her blood spurted against the wall and against the horses. Jehu trod over her body

text.NRSV: Then he went in and ate and drank; he said, “See to that cursed woman and bury her; for she is a king’s daughter.”
text.Rotherham: And, when he had entered and eaten and drunk, he said—Look, I pray you, after this accursed woman, and bury her, for the daughter of a king, she is.
text.KJV: 9:34 And when he was come in, hee did eate and drinke, and saide, Goe, see now this cursed woman, and burie her: for she is a kings daughter.
text.NABRE: 9:34 and, after eating and drinking, he said: “Attend to that accursed woman and bury her; for she was the daughter of a king.”

text.NRSV: But when they went to bury her, they found no more of her than the skull and the feet and the palms of her hands.
text.Rotherham: So they went to bury her,—but found not of her—save the skull, and the feet, and the palms of the hands.
text.KJV: 9:35 And they went to burie her, but they found no more of her then the skul, and the feete, & the palmes of her hands.
text.NABRE: 9:35 But when they went to bury her, they found nothing of her but the skull, the feet, and the hands.

text.NRSV: When they came back and told him, he said, “This is the word of the Lord, which he spoke by his servant Elijah the Tishbite, ‘In the territory of Jezreel the dogs shall eat the flesh of Jezebel;
text.Rotherham: Then came they back, and told him, and he said—The word of Yahweh, it is, which he spake by the hand of his servant, Elijah the Tishbite, saying—In the town-land of Jezreel, shall dogs eat the flesh of Jezebel!
text.KJV: 9:36 Wherefore they came againe, and told him: and he said, This is the word of the Lord, which he spake by his seruant Elijah the Tishbite, saying, In the portion of Iezreel shall dogs eate the flesh of Iezebel:
text.NABRE: 9:36 They returned to Jehu, and when they told him, he said, “This is the word the LORD spoke through his servant Elijah the Tishbite: In the confines of Jezreel the dogs shall devour the flesh of Jezebel.

text.NRSV: the corpse of Jezebel shall be like dung on the field in the territory of Jezreel, so that no one can say, This is Jezebel.’”
text.Rotherham: So shall the carcass of Jezebel become like heaps of dung on the face of the field, in the town-land of Jezreel: So that they cannot say, This, is Jezebel!
text.KJV: 9:37 And the carkeise of Iezebel shall be as doung vpon the face of the field in the portion of Iezreel, so that they shall not say, This is Iezebel.
text.NABRE: 9:37 The corpse of Jezebel shall be like dung in the field in the confines of Jezreel, so that no one can say: This was Jezebel.”

text.NRSV: Now Ahab had seventy sons in Samaria. So Jehu wrote letters and sent them to Samaria, to the rulers of Jezreel, to the elders, and to the guardians of the sons of Ahab, saying,
text.Rotherham: Now, Ahab, had seventy sons in Samaria,—so Jehu wrote letters, and sent to Samaria, unto the rulers of Jezreel—the elders, and unto them who had been foster-parents for Ahab, saying:
text.KJV: 10:1 And Ahab had seuentie sonnes in Samaria: and Iehu wrote letters, and sent to Samaria vnto the rulers of Iezreel, to the Elders, and to them that brought vp Ahabs children, saying,
text.NABRE: 10:1 Chapter 10 - Death of the Sons of Ahab of Israel. Ahab had seventy sons in Samaria. Jehu wrote letters and sent them to Samaria, to the elders who were rulers of Jezreel and to Ahab’s guardians. Jehu wrote:

text.NRSV: “Since your master’s sons are with you and you have at your disposal chariots and horses, a fortified city, and weapons,
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, when this letter cometh in unto you, there being, with you, the sons of your lord,—and, with you, the chariots and the homes, and a fortified city, and the armour,
text.KJV: 10:2 Now assoone as this letter commeth to you, seeing your masters sons are with you, and there are with you charets and horses, a fenced citie also, and armour:
text.NABRE: 10:2 “Since your master’s sons are with you, as well as his chariots, horses, fortified city, and weaponry, when this letter reaches you

text.NRSV: select the son of your master who is the best and most worthy, set him on his father’s throne, and fight for your master’s house.”
text.Rotherham: ye shall look out the goodliest and fittest of the sons of your lord, and set on the throne of his father, and ye shall do battle for the house of your lord.
text.KJV: 10:3 Looke euen out the best and meetest of your masters sonnes, and set him on his fathers throne, and fight for your masters house.
text.NABRE: 10:3 decide which is the best and the fittest of your master’s sons, place him on his father’s throne, and fight for your master’s house.”

text.NRSV: But they were utterly terrified and said, “Look, two kings could not stand before him; how then can we stand?”
text.Rotherham: Then feared they very greatly, and said, Lo! two kings, stood not before him; how then, should, we, stand?
text.KJV: 10:4 But they were exceedingly afraid, and said, Behold, two kings stood not before him: how then shall we stand?
text.NABRE: 10:4 They were overcome with fright and said, “If the two kings could not withstand him, how can we?”

text.NRSV: So the steward of the palace, and the governor of the city, along with the elders and the guardians, sent word to Jehu: “We are your servants; we will do anything you say. We will not make anyone king; do whatever you think right.”
text.Rotherham: So he that was over the house, and he that was over the city, and the elders, and the foster-parents sent unto Jehu, saying—Thy servants, we are! and, all that thou shalt say unto us, will we do,—we will make no man king, whatsoever is good in thine own eyes, do!
text.KJV: 10:5 And he that was ouer the house, and he that was ouer the citie, the elders also, and the bringers vp of the children, sent to Iehu, saying, Wee are thy seruants, and will doe all that thou shalt bid vs, we will not make any king: doe thou that which is good in thine eyes.
text.NABRE: 10:5 So the master of the palace and the chief of the city, along with the elders and the guardians, sent this message to Jehu: “We are your servants, and we will do everything you tell us. We will proclaim no one king; do whatever you think best.”

text.NRSV: Then he wrote them a second letter, saying, “If you are on my side, and if you are ready to obey me, take the heads of your master’s sons and come to me at Jezreel tomorrow about this time.” Now the king’s sons, seventy persons, were with the leaders of the city, who were rearing them.
text.Rotherham: Then wrote he unto them a second letter, saying—If, mine, ye are, and, unto my voice, ye intend to hearken, take ye the heads of the men who are sons of your lord, and come in unto me about this time to-morrow, in Jezreel. Now, the sons of the king, seventy persons, were with the great men of the city, who had been bringing them up.
text.KJV: 10:6 Then he wrote a letter the second time to them, saying, If yee be mine, and if ye will hearken vnto my voyce, take ye the heads of the men your masters sonnes, and come to me to Iezreel by to morow this time: (now the kings sonnes being seuenty persons, were with the great men of the city, which brought them vp.)
text.NABRE: 10:6 So Jehu wrote them a second letter: “If you are on my side and will obey me, bring along the heads of your master’s sons and come to me in Jezreel at this time tomorrow.” (The seventy princes were in the care of prominent men of the city, who were rearing them.)

text.NRSV: When the letter came to them, they took the king’s sons and killed them, seventy persons; they put their heads in baskets and sent them to him at Jezreel.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when the letter reached them, that they took the sons of the king, and slew them, seventy persons,—and put their heads in baskets, and sent unto him to Jezreel.
text.KJV: 10:7 And it came to passe when the letter came to them, that they tooke the kings sonnes, and slewe seuentie persons, and put their heads in baskets, and sent him them to Iezreel.
text.NABRE: 10:7 When the letter arrived, they took the princes and slew all seventy of them, put their heads in baskets, and sent them to Jehu in Jezreel.

text.NRSV: When the messenger came and told him, “They have brought the heads of the king’s sons,” he said, “Lay them in two heaps at the entrance of the gate until the morning.”
text.Rotherham: And there came in a messenger and told him, saying, They have brought in the heads of the sons of the king. And he said—Lay ye them in two heaps, at the entrance of the gate, until the morning.
text.KJV: 10:8 ¶ And there came a messenger, and tolde him, saying, They haue brought the heads of the kings sonnes. And he said, Lay ye them in two heaps at the entring in of the gate, vntill the morning.
text.NABRE: 10:8 A messenger came in and told him, “They have brought the heads of the princes.” He said, “Pile them in two heaps at the gate of the city until morning.”

text.NRSV: In the morning when he went out, he stood and said to all the people, “You are innocent. It was I who conspired against my master and killed him; but who struck down all these?
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, in the morning, that he went out and took his stand, and said unto all the people, Righteous, are, ye! Lo! I, conspired against my lord, and slew him, but, who, smote, all these?
text.KJV: 10:9 And it came to passe in the morning, that he went out, & stood, and said to all the people, Ye be righteous: behold, I conspired against my master, and slew him: But who slew all these?
text.NABRE: 10:9 In the morning he came outside, stood there, and said to all the people: “You are guiltless, for it was I who conspired against my lord and slew him. But who killed all these?

text.NRSV: Know then that there shall fall to the earth nothing of the word of the Lord, which the Lord spoke concerning the house of Ahab; for the Lord has done what he said through his servant Elijah.”
text.Rotherham: Know ye, then, that there shall fall nought of the word of Yahweh, to the ground, which Yahweh spake concerning the house of Ahab,—but, Yahweh, hath done that which he spake through his servant Elijah.
text.KJV: 10:10 Know now, that there shall fall vnto the earth nothing of the worde of the Lord, which the Lord spake concerning the house of Ahab: for the Lord hath done that which he spake by his seruant Elijah.
text.NABRE: 10:10 Know that not a single word which the LORD has spoken against the house of Ahab shall fail. The LORD has accomplished what he decreed through his servant Elijah.”

text.NRSV: So Jehu struck down all who were left of the house of Ahab in Jezreel, all his leaders, close friends, and priests, until he left him no survivor.
text.Rotherham: Then Jehu smote all that were left remaining unto the house of Ahab, in Jezreel, and all his great men, and his acquaintances and his priests,—until there was not left remaining to him, a survivor.
text.KJV: 10:11 So Iehu slew all that remained of the house of Ahab, in Iezreel, and all his great men, and his kinsefolkes, and his priests, vntill he left him none remaining.
text.NABRE: 10:11 (And so Jehu slew all who were left of the house of Ahab in Jezreel, as well as all his powerful supporters, intimates, and priests, leaving him no survivor.)

text.NRSV: Then he set out and went to Samaria. On the way, at Beth-eked of the Shepherds,
text.Rotherham: Then arose he, and came in, and departed for Samaria,—the sheep-shearing house itself being on the road;
text.KJV: 10:12 ¶ And hee arose, and departed, and came to Samaria: And as he was at the shearing house in the way,
text.NABRE: 10:12 Then he went back inside. Death of the Relatives of Ahaziah of Judah. He set out for Samaria and, at Beth-eked-haroim on the way,

text.NRSV: Jehu met relatives of King Ahaziah of Judah and said, “Who are you?” They answered, “We are relatives of Ahaziah; we have come down to visit the royal princes and the sons of the queen mother.”
text.Rotherham: so, Jehu, lighted upon the brethren of Ahaziah king of Judah, and said, Who are, ye? And they said—Brethren of Ahaziah, are, we; so we came down to salute the sons of the king, and the sons of the queen.
text.KJV: 10:13 Iehu met with the brethren of Ahaziah king of Iudah, and said, Who are ye? And they answered, Wee are the brethren of Ahaziah, and we go downe to salute the children of the King, and the children of the Queene.
text.NABRE: 10:13 Jehu came across relatives of Ahaziah, king of Judah. “Who are you?” he asked, and they said, “We are relatives of Ahaziah. We are going down to visit the princes and the family of the queen mother.”

text.NRSV: He said, “Take them alive.” They took them alive and slaughtered them at the pit of Beth-eked, forty-two in all; he spared none of them.
text.Rotherham: Then said he,—Take them alive. So they took them alive, and slew them [casting them] into the pit of the shearing house,—forty-two men, neither left he remaining a man of them.
text.KJV: 10:14 And hee said, Take them aliue. And they tooke them aliue, and slew them at the pit of the shearing house, euen two and fourty men; neither left he any of them.
text.NABRE: 10:14 “Take them alive,” Jehu ordered. They were taken alive, forty-two in number, then slain at the pit of Beth-eked. Not one of them survived.

text.NRSV: When he left there, he met Jehonadab son of Rechab coming to meet him; he greeted him, and said to him, “Is your heart as true to mine as mine is to yours?” Jehonadab answered, “It is.” Jehu said, “If it is, give me your hand.” So he gave him his hand. Jehu took him up with him into the chariot,
text.Rotherham: Then departed he from thence, and lighted on Jehonadab son of Rechab coming to meet him, and he blessed him, and said unto him—Is thy heart, right, as my heart is with thy heart? And Jehonadab said—It is. Then, if it is, give me thy hand. So he gave him his hand. And he took him up to him, into the chariot;
text.KJV: 10:15 ¶ And when hee was departed thence, he lighted on Iehonadab the sonne of Rechab, comming to meet him: and he saluted him, & said to him, Is thine heart right, as my heart is with thy heart? And Iehonadab answered, It is: If it be, giue mee thine hand. And hee gaue him his hand, and hee tooke him vp to him into the charet.
text.NABRE: 10:15 When he set out from there, Jehu met Jehonadab, son of Rechab, on the road. He greeted him and asked, “Are you with me wholeheartedly, as I am with you?” “Yes,” he replied. “If you are, give me your hand.” He gave him his hand, and he had him mount his chariot,

text.NRSV: and said, “Come with me, and see my zeal for the Lord.” So he had him ride in his chariot.
text.Rotherham: and said—Do come with me, and see my jealousy for Yahweh. So he made him ride in his chariot.
text.KJV: 10:16 And he said, Come with me, and see my zeale for the Lord: so they made him ride in his charet.
text.NABRE: 10:16 and said, “Come with me and see my zeal for the LORD.” And they took him along in his chariot.

text.NRSV: When he came to Samaria, he killed all who were left to Ahab in Samaria, until he had wiped them out, according to the word of the Lord that he spoke to Elijah.
text.Rotherham: And, when he came into Samaria, he smote all that were left remaining unto Ahab, in Samaria, until he had destroyed him,—according to the word of Yahweh, which he had spoken unto Elijah.
text.KJV: 10:17 And when he came to Samaria, he slew all that remained vnto Ahab in Samaria, till he had destroyed him, according to the saying of the Lord, which he spake to Eliiah.
text.NABRE: 10:17 Slaughter of the Worshipers of Baal. When he arrived in Samaria, Jehu slew all who were left of Ahab’s line in Samaria, doing away with them completely, according to the word the LORD spoke to Elijah.

text.NRSV: Then Jehu assembled all the people and said to them, “Ahab offered Baal a little service; Jehu will offer much.”
text.Rotherham: Then Jehu gathered together all the people, and said unto them,—Ahab, served Baal, a little,—Jehu, will serve him, much.
text.KJV: 10:18 ¶ And Iehu gathered all the people together, and said vnto them, Ahab serued Baal a litle, but Iehu shall serue him much.
text.NABRE: 10:18 Jehu gathered all the people together and said to them: “Ahab served Baal to some extent, but Jehu will serve him yet more.

text.NRSV: Now therefore summon to me all the prophets of Baal, all his worshippers, and all his priests; let none be missing, for I have a great sacrifice to offer to Baal; whoever is missing shall not live. But Jehu was acting with guile in order to destroy the worshippers of Baal.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, all the prophets of Baal, all his servants and all his priests, call ye for me; do not let, a man, be missing; for, a great sacrifice, have I, to Baal, no one that is missing, shall live. But, Jehu, acted, craftily, to the end he might destroy the servants of Baal.
text.KJV: 10:19 Now therefore, call vnto me all the prophets of Baal, all his seruants, and all his priests, let none be wanting: for I haue a great sacrifice to doe to Baal; whosoeuer shall be wanting, he shall not liue. But Iehu did it in subtilitie, to the intent that hee might destroy the worshippers of Baal.
text.NABRE: 10:19 Now summon for me all Baal’s prophets, all his servants, and all his priests. See that no one is absent, for I have a great sacrifice for Baal. Whoever is absent shall not live.” This Jehu did as a ruse, so that he might destroy the servants of Baal.

text.NRSV: Jehu decreed, “Sanctify a solemn assembly for Baal.” So they proclaimed it.
text.Rotherham: And Jehu said—Hallow ye a solemn festival unto Baal. And they made proclamation.
text.KJV: 10:20 And Iehu said, Proclaime a solemne assembly for Baal. And they proclaimed it.
text.NABRE: 10:20 Jehu said further, “Proclaim a solemn assembly in honor of Baal.” They did so,

text.NRSV: Jehu sent word throughout all Israel; all the worshippers of Baal came, so that there was no one left who did not come. They entered the temple of Baal, until the temple of Baal was filled from wall to wall.
text.Rotherham: And Jehu sent throughout all Israel, and all the servants of Baal came in, so that there was not left remaining a man, who had not come in,—and they entered the house of Baal, and the house of Baal was filled, from door to door.
text.KJV: 10:21 And Iehu sent through all Israel, and all the worshippers of Baal came, so that there was not a man left that came not: and they came into the house of Baal; and the house of Baal was full from one end to another.
text.NABRE: 10:21 and Jehu sent word of it throughout all Israel. All the servants of Baal came; there was no one who did not come; they came to the temple of Baal, and it was filled from wall to wall.

text.NRSV: He said to the keeper of the wardrobe, “Bring out the vestments for all the worshippers of Baal.” So he brought out the vestments for them.
text.Rotherham: Then said he to him who was over the wardrobe chamber, Bring forth vestments for all the servants of Baal. And he brought forth for them the vestments.
text.KJV: 10:22 And he said vnto him that was ouer the vestrie, Bring forth vestments for all the worshippers of Baal. And he brought them forth vestments.
text.NABRE: 10:22 Then Jehu said to the custodian of the wardrobe, “Bring out garments for all the servants of Baal.” When he had brought out the garments for them,

text.NRSV: Then Jehu went into the temple of Baal with Jehonadab son of Rechab; and he said to the worshippers of Baal, “Search and see that there is no worshipper of the Lord here among you, but only worshippers of Baal.”
text.Rotherham: Then entered Jehu, with Jehonadab son of Rechab, into the house of Baal,—and he said unto the servants of Baal—Search ye and see, that there be not here, with you, any of the servants of Yahweh, none but the servants of Baal, alone.
text.KJV: 10:23 And Iehu went, and Iehonadab the sonne of Rechab into the house of Baal, and said vnto the worshippers of Baal, Search, and looke that there be here with you none of the seruants of the Lord, but the worshippers of Baal onely.
text.NABRE: 10:23 Jehu, with Jehonadab, son of Rechab, entered the temple of Baal and said to the servants of Baal, “Search and be sure that there is no one who serves the LORD here with you, but only servants of Baal.”

text.NRSV: Then they proceeded to offer sacrifices and burnt offerings. Now Jehu had stationed eighty men outside, saying, “Whoever allows any of those to escape whom I deliver into your hands shall forfeit his life.”
text.Rotherham: And, when they entered to offer sacrifices and ascending-offerings, Jehu, set him, outside, eighty men, and said—The man who shall escape, of the men whom I am bringing into your power, his own life, shall be for, his life.
text.KJV: 10:24 And when they went in to offer sacrifices, and burnt offerings, Iehu appointed fourescore men without, and said, If any of the men whom I haue brought into your hands, escape, hee that letteth him goe, his life shall be for the life of him.
text.NABRE: 10:24 Then they proceeded to offer sacrifices and burnt offerings. Now Jehu had stationed eighty troops outside with this warning, “Any of you who lets someone escape of those whom I shall deliver into your hands shall pay life for life.”

text.NRSV: As soon as he had finished presenting the burnt offering, Jehu said to the guards and to the officers, “Come in and kill them; let no one escape.” So they put them to the sword. The guards and the officers threw them out, and then went into the inner shrine of the temple of Baal.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, as soon as he had made an end of offering the ascending-sacrifice, that Jehu said to the runners and to the officers—Enter, smite them, let not, a man, get out. So they smote them, with the edge of the sword,—and the runners and the officers cast them out, and then went as far as the city of the house of Baal,
text.KJV: 10:25 And it came to passe assoone as hee had made an end of offering the burnt offering, that Iehu saide to the guard, and to the captaines, Goe in, and slay them, let none come foorth. And they smote them with the edge of the sword, and the guard, and the captaines cast them out, and went to the citie of the house of Baal.
text.NABRE: 10:25 As soon as he finished offering the burnt offering, Jehu said to the guards and aides, “Go in and slay them. Let no one escape.” So the guards and aides put them to the sword and cast them out. Afterward they went into the inner shrine of the temple of Baal,

text.NRSV: They brought out the pillar that was in the temple of Baal, and burned it.
text.Rotherham: and brought forth the idolatrous pillars that were in the house of Baal, and then burned it;
text.KJV: 10:26 And they brought foorth the Images out of the house of Baal, and burnt them.
text.NABRE: 10:26 and took out the pillars of the temple of Baal. They burned the shrine,

text.NRSV: They demolished the pillar of Baal, and demolished the temple of Baal, and made it a latrine to this day.
text.Rotherham: and they brake down the pillars of Baal,—and brake down the house of Baal, and appointed it for a sewer-house—until this day.
text.KJV: 10:27 And they brake downe the image of Baal, and brake downe the house of Baal, and made it a draughthouse, vnto this day.
text.NABRE: 10:27 tore down the pillar of Baal, tore down the temple of Baal, and turned it into a latrine, as it remains today.

text.NRSV: Thus Jehu wiped out Baal from Israel.
text.Rotherham: Thus Jehu destroyed Baal out of Israel.
text.KJV: 10:28 Thus Iehu destroyed Baal out of Israel.
text.NABRE: 10:28 Thus Jehu destroyed Baal in Israel.

text.NRSV: But Jehu did not turn aside from the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, which he caused Israel to commit—the golden calves that were in Bethel and in Dan.
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless, as for the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, which he caused, Israel, to commit, Jehu turned not away from following them,—the calves of gold, one being in Bethel, and the other in Dan.
text.KJV: 10:29 ¶ Howbeit, from the sinnes of Ieroboam the sonne of Nebat, who made Israel to sinne, Iehu departed not from after them, to wit, the golden calues that were in Bethel, and that were in Dan.
text.NABRE: 10:29 Death of Jehu of Israel. However, Jehu did not desist from the sins which Jeroboam, son of Nebat, had caused Israel to commit, the golden calves at Bethel and at Dan.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Jehu, “Because you have done well in carrying out what is right in my eyes, and have done to the house of Ahab all that was in my heart, your sons of the fourth generation shall sit on the throne of Israel.”
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto Jehu: Because thou hast done well, by doing that which was right in mine eyes—according to all that was in my heart, hast done to the house of Ahab, sons, of thine, unto the fourth generation, shall sit upon the throne of Israel.
text.KJV: 10:30 And the Lord said vnto Iehu, Because thou hast done well in executing that which is right in mine eyes, and hast done vnto the house of Ahab according to all that was in mine heart, thy children of the fourth generation, shal sit on the throne of Israel.
text.NABRE: 10:30 The LORD said to Jehu: Because you have done well what is right in my eyes, and have done to the house of Ahab all that was in my heart, your sons to the fourth generation shall sit upon the throne of Israel.

text.NRSV: But Jehu was not careful to follow the law of the Lord the God of Israel with all his heart; he did not turn from the sins of Jeroboam, which he caused Israel to commit.
text.Rotherham: But, Jehu, took not heed to walk in the law of Yahweh God of Israel, with all his heart,—he turned not away from the sins of Jeroboam, which he caused, Israel, to commit.
text.KJV: 10:31 But Iehu tooke no heede to walke in the Law of the Lord God of Israel, with all his heart: for he departed not from the sinnes of Ieroboam, which made Israel to sinne.
text.NABRE: 10:31 But Jehu was not careful to walk in the law of the LORD, the God of Israel, with all his heart, since he did not desist from the sins which Jeroboam had caused Israel to commit.

text.NRSV: In those days the Lord began to trim off parts of Israel. Hazael defeated them throughout the territory of Israel:
text.Rotherham: In those days, began Yahweh to make inroads in Israel,—and Hazael smote them in all the boundaries of Israel;
text.KJV: 10:32 ¶ In those dayes the Lord began to cut Israel short: and Hazael smote them in all the coasts of Israel:
text.NABRE: 10:32 At that time the LORD began to dismember Israel. Hazael defeated the Israelites throughout their territory

text.NRSV: from the Jordan eastward, all the land of Gilead, the Gadites, the Reubenites, and the Manassites, from Aroer, which is by the Wadi Arnon, that is, Gilead and Bashan.
text.Rotherham: from the Jordan, towards sunrise, all the land of Gilead, the Gadites, and the Reubenites, and the Manassites,—from Aroer, which is by the torrent of Arnon, both Gilead and Bashan.
text.KJV: 10:33 From Iordan Eastward, all the land of Gilead, the Gadites, and the Reubenites, and the Manassites, from Aroer, (which is by the riuer Arnon) euen Gilead and Bashan.
text.NABRE: 10:33 east of the Jordan (all the land of Gilead, of the Gadites, Reubenites, and Manassites), from Aroer on the wadi Arnon up through Gilead and Bashan.

text.NRSV: Now the rest of the acts of Jehu, all that he did, and all his power, are they not written in the Book of the Annals of the Kings of Israel?
text.Rotherham: Now, the rest of the story of Jehu, and all that he did, and all his might, are they not written in the book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Israel?
text.KJV: 10:34 Now the rest of the acts of Iehu, and all that he did, & all his might, are they not written in the booke of the Chronicles of the kings of Israel?
text.NABRE: 10:34 The rest of the acts of Jehu, with all that he did and all his valor, are recorded in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel.

text.NRSV: So Jehu slept with his ancestors, and they buried him in Samaria. His son Jehoahaz succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: And Jehu slept with his fathers, and they buried him in Samaria,—and, Jehoahaz his son, reigned, in his stead.
text.KJV: 10:35 And Iehu slept with his fathers, and they buried him in Samaria, and Iehoahaz his sonne reigned in his stead.
text.NABRE: 10:35 Jehu rested with his ancestors and was buried in Samaria, and his son Jehoahaz succeeded him as king.

text.NRSV: The time that Jehu reigned over Israel in Samaria was twenty-eight years.
text.Rotherham: Now, the days that Jehu reigned over Israel, were twenty-eight years, in Samaria.
text.KJV: 10:36 And the time that Iehu reigned ouer Israel in Samaria, was twentie and eight yeeres.
text.NABRE: 10:36 The length of Jehu’s reign over Israel was twenty-eight years in Samaria.

text.NRSV: Now when Athaliah, Ahaziah’s mother, saw that her son was dead, she set about to destroy all the royal family.
text.Rotherham: Now, when, Athaliah, mother of Ahaziah, saw that her son was dead, she arose and destroyed all the seed royal.
text.KJV: 11:1 And when Athaliah the mother of Ahaziah sawe that her sonne was dead, she arose, and destroyed all the seed royall.
text.NABRE: 11:1 Chapter 11 - Death of the Heirs of Ahaziah of Judah. When Athaliah, the mother of Ahaziah, saw that her son was dead, she began to kill off the whole royal family.

text.NRSV: But Jehosheba, King Joram’s daughter, Ahaziah’s sister, took Joash son of Ahaziah, and stole him away from among the king’s sons who were about to be killed; she put him and his nurse in a bedroom. Thus she hid him from Athaliah, so that he was not killed;
text.Rotherham: But Jehosheba daughter of King Joram, sister of Ahaziah, took Joash son of Ahaziah, and stole him from among the king’s sons that were being slain—him and his nurse, into the bedchamber,—so she hid him from the face of Athaliah, that he was not slain.
text.KJV: 11:2 But Iehosheba the daughter of king Ioram, sister of Ahaziah, tooke Ioash the sonne of Ahaziah, and stale him from among the Kings sonnes which were slaine; and they hid him, euen him and his nurse in the bed-chamber from Athaliah, so that he was not slaine.
text.NABRE: 11:2 But Jehosheba, daughter of King Joram and sister of Ahaziah, took Joash, Ahaziah’s son, and spirited him away, along with his nurse, from the bedroom where the princes were about to be slain. He was concealed from Athaliah, and so he did not die.

text.NRSV: he remained with her six years, hidden in the house of the Lord, while Athaliah reigned over the land.
text.Rotherham: And he was with her, in the house of Yahweh, hiding himself, six years,—while, Athaliah, was reigning over the land.
text.KJV: 11:3 And he was with her hidde in the House of the Lord, sixe yeeres: and Athaliah did reigne ouer the land.
text.NABRE: 11:3 For six years he remained hidden with her in the house of the LORD, while Athaliah ruled as queen over the land.

text.NRSV: But in the seventh year Jehoiada summoned the captains of the Carites and of the guards and had them come to him in the house of the Lord. He made a covenant with them and put them under oath in the house of the Lord; then he showed them the king’s son.
text.Rotherham: But, in the seventh year, Jehoiada sent and took the captains over hundreds, of the Carian body-guard, and of the runners, and brought them unto him, in the house of Yahweh,—and, when he had solemnised a Covenant with them, and put them on oath, in the house of Yahweh, then shewed he them the king’s son;
text.KJV: 11:4 ¶ And the seuenth yeere Iehoiada sent and fet the rulers ouer hundreds, with the captains, and the guard, and brought them to him into the house of the Lord, and made a couenant with them, and tooke an othe of them in the house of the Lord, and shewed them the Kings sonne.
text.NABRE: 11:4 Death of Athaliah. But in the seventh year, Jehoiada summoned the captains of the Carians and of the guards. He had them come to him in the house of the LORD, made a covenant with them, exacted an oath from them in the house of the LORD, and then showed them the king’s son.

text.NRSV: He commanded them, “This is what you are to do: one-third of you, those who go off duty on the sabbath and guard the king’s house
text.Rotherham: and commanded them, saying—This, is the thing which ye must do,—A third part of you, will be coming in on the sabbath, and keeping the watch of the house of the king;
text.KJV: 11:5 And he commanded them, saying, This is the thing that yee shall doe; A third part of you that enter in on the Sabbath, shall euen be keepers of the watch of the kings house:
text.NABRE: 11:5 He gave them these orders: “This is what you must do: one third of you who come on duty on the sabbath shall guard the king’s house;

text.NRSV: (another third being at the gate Sur and a third at the gate behind the guards), shall guard the palace;
text.Rotherham: and, a third, in the side-gate; and, a third, in the gate behind the runners,—so shall ye keep the watch of the house, by turns.
text.KJV: 11:6 And a third part shall be at the gate of Sur, and a third part at the gate behinde the guard: so shall yee keepe the watch of the house, that it be not broken downe.
text.NABRE: 11:6 another third shall be at the gate Sur; and the last third shall be at the gate behind the guards. You shall guard the palace on all sides,

text.NRSV: and your two divisions that come on duty on the sabbath and guard the house of the Lord of the king’s behalf
text.Rotherham: And, two parts of you, are all that are going out on the sabbath,—so shall ye keep the watch of the house of Yahweh, as touching the king.
text.KJV: 11:7 And two parts of all you, that goe foorth on the Sabbath, euen they shall keepe the watch of the house of the Lord about the King.
text.NABRE: 11:7 while the two of your divisions who are going off duty that week shall keep guard over the house of the LORD for the king.

text.NRSV: shall surround the king, each with weapons in hand; and whoever approaches the ranks is to be killed. Be with the king in his going out and his coming in.”
text.Rotherham: Thus shall ye encompass the king round about, every man with his weapons in his hand, and, he that cometh within the ranks, let him be slain,—so be ye with the king, when he cometh out and when he goeth in.
text.KJV: 11:8 And yee shall compasse the King round about, euery man with his weapons in his hand: and he that commeth within the ranges, let him bee slaine: and be yee with the king, as hee goeth out, and as he commeth in.
text.NABRE: 11:8 You shall surround the king, each with drawn weapons, and anyone who tries to approach the guard detail is to be killed; stay with the king, wherever he goes.”

text.NRSV: The captains did according to all that the priest Jehoiada commanded; each brought his men who were to go off duty on the sabbath, with those who were to come on duty on the sabbath, and came to the priest Jehoiada.
text.Rotherham: And the captains of hundreds did according to all that Jehoiada the priest commanded, and they took every man his men who were coming in on the sabbath, with them who were going out on the sabbath,—and came unto Jehoiada the priest.
text.KJV: 11:9 And the captaines ouer the hundreds did according to all things that Iehoiada the Priest commanded: and they tooke euery man his men that were to come in on the Sabbath, with them that should goe out on the Sabbath, and came to Iehoiada the Priest.
text.NABRE: 11:9 The captains did just as Jehoiada the priest commanded. Each took his troops, both those going on duty for the week and those going off duty that week, and came to Jehoiada the priest.

text.NRSV: The priest delivered to the captains the spears and shields that had been King David’s, which were in the house of the Lord;
text.Rotherham: And the priest gave unto the captains of hundreds the spears and the shields which belonged to King David,—which were in the house of Yahweh.
text.KJV: 11:10 And to the captaines ouer hundreds, did the Priest giue king Dauids speares and shields, that were in the Temple.
text.NABRE: 11:10 He gave the captains King David’s spear and quivers, which were in the house of the LORD.

text.NRSV: the guards stood, every man with his weapons in his hand, from the south side of the house to the north side of the house, around the altar and the house, to guard the king on every side.
text.Rotherham: And the runners stood, every man with his weapons in his hand, from the right corner of the house, as far as the left corner of the house, by the altar and the house,—near the king, round about.
text.KJV: 11:11 And the guard stood, euery man with his weapons in his hand, round about the king, from the right corner of the Temple, to the left corner of the Temple, along by the Altar and the Temple.
text.NABRE: 11:11 And the guards, with drawn weapons, lined up from the southern to the northern limit of the enclosure, surrounding the altar and the temple on the king’s behalf.

text.NRSV: Then he brought out the king’s son, put the crown on him, and gave him the covenant; they proclaimed him king, and anointed him; they clapped their hands and shouted, “Long live the king!”
text.Rotherham: Then brought he forth the king’s son, and set upon him the crown, and the testimony, and they made him king, and anointed him,—and clapped their hands, and said—May the king live!
text.KJV: 11:12 And he brought foorth the kings sonne, and put the crowne vpon him, and gaue him the Testimonie, and they made him King, and anointed him, and they clapt their hands, and said, God saue the King.
text.NABRE: 11:12 Then Jehoiada brought out the king’s son and put the crown and the testimony upon him. They proclaimed him king and anointed him, clapping their hands and shouting, “Long live the king!”

text.NRSV: When Athaliah heard the noise of the guard and of the people, she went into the house of the Lord to the people;
text.Rotherham: And, when Athaliah heard the noise of the runners, the people, then came she in unto the people, in the house of Yahweh,
text.KJV: 11:13 ¶ And when Athaliah heard the noise of the guard, and of the people, she came to the people, into the Temple of the Lord.
text.NABRE: 11:13 When Athaliah heard the noise made by the people, she came before them in the house of the LORD.

text.NRSV: when she looked, there was the king standing by the pillar, according to custom, with the captains and the trumpeters beside the king, and all the people of the land rejoicing and blowing trumpets. Then Athaliah tore her clothes and cried, “Treason! Treason!”
text.Rotherham: and looked, and lo! the king, standing by the pillar, as the custom was, and the captains and the trumpeters, by the king, and, all the people of the land, rejoicing, and blowing with trumpets,—so Athaliah rent her garments, and cried out—Conspiracy! conspiracy!
text.KJV: 11:14 And when shee looked, behold, the king stood by a pillar, as the maner was, and the Princes, and the trumpetters by the King, and all the people of the land reioyced, and blew with trumpets: and Athaliah rent her clothes, and cryed, Treason, treason.
text.NABRE: 11:14 When she saw the king standing by the column, as was the custom, and the captains and trumpeters near the king, and all the people of the land rejoicing and blowing trumpets, Athaliah tore her garments and cried out, “Treason, treason!”

text.NRSV: Then the priest Jehoiada commanded the captains who were set over the army, “Bring her out between the ranks, and kill with the sword anyone who follows her.” For the priest said, “Let her not be killed in the house of the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: And Jehoiada the priest commanded the captains of hundreds—officers of the force, and said unto them—Take her forth into the inside of the ranks, and, he that cometh in after her, [ye are] to slay with the sword. For the priest said, Let her not be slain, in the house of Yahweh.
text.KJV: 11:15 But Iehoiada the Priest commanded the captaines of the hundreds, the officers of the hoste, and sayde vnto them, Haue her foorth without the ranges; and him that followeth her, kill with the sword: for the Priest had sayd, Let her not be slaine in the house of the Lord.
text.NABRE: 11:15 Then Jehoiada the priest instructed the captains in command of the force: “Escort her with a guard detail. If anyone follows her, let him die by the sword.” For the priest had said, “She must not die in the house of the LORD.”

text.NRSV: So they laid hands on her; she went through the horses’ entrance to the king’s house, and there she was killed.
text.Rotherham: So they made way for her, and she entered the road by which the horses approached the house of the king, and was slain there.
text.KJV: 11:16 And they laid hands on her, and she went by the way, by the which the horses came into the kings house, and there was she slaine.
text.NABRE: 11:16 So they seized her, and when she reached the Horse Gate of the king’s house, she was put to death.

text.NRSV: Jehoiada made a covenant between the Lord and the king and people, that they should be the Lord’s people; also between the king and the people.
text.Rotherham: And Jehoiada solemnised a covenant between Yahweh, and the king, and the people, that they should become a people unto Yahweh,—also between the king and the people.
text.KJV: 11:17 ¶ And Iehoiada made a couenant betweene the Lord and the king, and the people, that they should be the Lords people; betweene the king also and the people.
text.NABRE: 11:17 Then Jehoiada made a covenant between the LORD and the king and the people, by which they would be the LORD’s people; and another between the king and the people.

text.NRSV: Then all the people of the land went to the house of Baal, and demolished it; his altars and his images they broke in pieces, and they killed Mattan, the priest of Baal, before the altars. The priest appointed guards over the house of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And all the people of the land entered the house of Baal, and brake it down, his altars and his images, brake they in pieces, utterly, and, Mattan the priest of Baal, they slew before the altars,—and the priest appointed officers over the house of Yahweh.
text.KJV: 11:18 And all the people of the land went into the house of Baal, and brake it down, his altars, and his images brake they in pieces throughly, and slew Mattan the priest of Baal before the altars: and the Priest appointed officers ouer the house of the Lord.
text.NABRE: 11:18 Thereupon all the people of the land went to the temple of Baal and demolished it. They shattered its altars and images completely, and slew Mattan, the priest of Baal, before the altars. Jehoiada the priest appointed a detachment for the house of the LORD,

text.NRSV: He took the captains, the Carites, the guards, and all the people of the land; then they brought the king down from the house of the Lord, marching through the gate of the guards to the king’s house. He took his seat on the throne of the kings.
text.Rotherham: And he took the captains of hundreds and the Carian bodyguard, and the runners, and all the people of the land, and they brought down the king out of the house of Yahweh, and they came, by way of the gate of the runners, into the house of the king,—and he took his seat on the throne of the kings;
text.KJV: 11:19 And hee tooke the rulers ouer hundreds, and the captaines, and the guard, and all the people of the land, and they brought downe the king from the house of the Lord, and came by the way of the gate of the guard, to the kings house, and he sate on the throne of the kings.
text.NABRE: 11:19 and took the captains, the Carians, the guards, and all the people of the land, and they led the king down from the house of the LORD; they came through the guards’ gate to the king’s house, and Joash took his seat on the royal throne.

text.NRSV: So all the people of the land rejoiced; and the city was quiet after Athaliah had been killed with the sword at the king’s house.
text.Rotherham: and all the people of the land rejoiced, and, the city, had rest,—when they had put, Athaliah, to death with the sword, in the house of the king.
text.KJV: 11:20 And all the people of the land reioyced, and the citie was in quiet, and they slew Athaliah with the sword, beside the kings house.
text.NABRE: 11:20 All the people of the land rejoiced and the city was quiet, now that Athaliah had been slain with the sword at the king’s house.

text.NRSV: Jehoash was seven years old when he began to reign.
text.Rotherham: Seven years old, was Jehoash, when he began to reign.
text.KJV: 11:21 Seuen yeeres old was Iehoash when he began to reigne.

text.NRSV: In the seventh year of Jehu, Jehoash began to reign; he reigned forty years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Zibiah of Beer-sheba.
text.Rotherham: In the seventh year of Jehu, began Jehoash to reign, and, forty years, reigned he in Jerusalem,—and, the name of his mother, was Zibiah of Beer-sheba.
text.KJV: 12:1 In the seuenth yeere of Iehu, Iehoash began to reigne, and fourtie yeeres reigned he in Ierusalem, and his mothers name was Zibiah of Beersheba.
text.NABRE: 12:1 IX. Kings of Judah and Israel Chapter 12 - Reign of Joash of Judah. Joash was seven years old when he became king.

text.NRSV: Jehoash did what was right in the sight of the Lord all his days, because the priest Jehoiada instructed him.
text.Rotherham: And Jehoash did that which was right in the eyes of Yahweh all his days,—whereunto, Jehoiada the priest, instructed him:
text.KJV: 12:2 And Iehoash did that which was right in the sight of the Lord all his dayes, wherein Iehoiada the Priest instructed him.
text.NABRE: 12:2 In the seventh year of Jehu, Joash became king, and he reigned forty years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Zibiah, from Beer-sheba.

text.NRSV: Nevertheless the high places were not removed; the people continued to sacrifice and make offerings on the high places.
text.Rotherham: save only, that, the high places, took they not away,—still were the people sacrificing and burning incense in the high places.
text.KJV: 12:3 But the high places were not taken away: the people still sacrificed, and burnt incense in the high places.
text.NABRE: 12:3 Joash did what was right in the LORD’s sight as long as he lived, because Jehoiada the priest guided him,

text.NRSV: Jehoash said to the priests, “All the money offered as sacred donations that is brought into the house of the Lord, the money for which each person is assessed—the money from the assessment of persons—and the money from the voluntary offerings brought into the house of the Lord,
text.Rotherham: And Jehoash said unto the priests,—All the silver of the hallowed things, that is brought into the house of Yahweh—the silver of one who transgresseth, the silver of [their] persons by the estimate of, each one,—all the silver which it cometh into any man’s heart to bring into the house of Yahweh,
text.KJV: 12:4 ¶ And Iehoash said to the priests, All the money of the dedicated things that is brought into the house of the Lord, euen the money of euery one that passeth the account, the money that euery man is set at, and all the money that commeth into any mans heart, to bring into the house of the Lord,
text.NABRE: 12:4 though the high places did not disappear; the people continued to sacrifice and to burn incense on the high places.

text.NRSV: let the priests receive from each of the donors; and let them repair the house wherever any need of repair is discovered.”
text.Rotherham: let the priests take to them, every one from his acquaintance,—and let, them, repair the breaches of the house, wheresoever there may be found a breach.
text.KJV: 12:5 Let the priests take it to them, euery man of his acquaintance, and let them repaire the breaches of the house, wheresoeuer any breach shalbe found.
text.NABRE: 12:5 Joash said to the priests: “All the funds for sacred purposes that are brought to the house of the LORD—the census tax, personal redemption money—and all funds that are freely brought to the house of the LORD,

text.NRSV: But by the twenty-third year of King Jehoash the priests had made no repairs on the house.
text.Rotherham: But it came to pass, that, in the twenty-third year of King Jehoash, the priests had not repaired the breaches of the house.
text.KJV: 12:6 But it was so that in the three and twentieth yeere of king Iehoash, the priests had not repaired the breaches of the house.
text.NABRE: 12:6 the priests may take for themselves, each from his own vendor. However, they must make whatever repairs on the temple may prove necessary.”

text.NRSV: Therefore King Jehoash summoned the priest Jehoiada with the other priests and said to them, “Why are you not repairing the house? Now therefore do not accept any more money from your donors but deliver it for the repair of the house.”
text.Rotherham: So King Jehoash called for Jehoiada the priest, and for the other priests, and said unto them—Why are ye not repairing the breaches of the house? Now, therefore, do not take silver from your acquaintances, for, to [repair] the breaches of the house, ought ye to have given it?
text.KJV: 12:7 Then king Iehoash called for Iehoiada the priest, and the other priests, and saide vnto them, Why repaire ye not the breaches of the house? now therefore receiue no more money of your acquaintance, but deliuer it for the breaches of the house.
text.NABRE: 12:7 Nevertheless, as late as the twenty-third year of the reign of King Joash, the priests had not made needed repairs on the temple.

text.NRSV: So the priests agreed that they would neither accept more money from the people nor be in charge of the repairs of the house.
text.Rotherham: The priests therefore consented, not to take silver from the people, and not to repair the breaches of the house.
text.KJV: 12:8 And the priests consented to receiue no more money of the people, neither to repaire the breaches of the house.
text.NABRE: 12:8 Accordingly, King Joash summoned the priest Jehoiada and the other priests. He asked, “Why do you not repair the temple? You must no longer take funds from your vendors, but you shall turn them over for the repairs.”

text.NRSV: Then the priest Jehoiada took a chest, made a hole in its lid, and set it beside the altar on the right side as one entered the house of the Lord; the priests who guarded the threshold put in it all the money that was brought into the house of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Then took Jehoiada a certain chest, and bored a hole in the door thereof,—and set it beside the altar, on the right as one entereth into the house of Yahweh, and the priests that kept the entrance-hall, used to put therein—all the silver that was brought into the house of Yahweh.
text.KJV: 12:9 But Iehoiada the priest tooke a chest, and bored a hole in the lid of it, and set it beside the Altar, on the right side, as one commeth into the house of the Lord, and the priests that kept the doore, put therein all the money that was brought into the house of the Lord.
text.NABRE: 12:9 So the priests agreed that they would neither take funds from the people nor make the repairs on the temple.

text.NRSV: Whenever they saw that there was a great deal of money in the chest, the king’s secretary and the high priest went up, counted the money that was found in the house of the Lord, and tied it up in bags.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when they saw that there was much silver in the chest, that the king’s scribe and the high priest came up, and brought together and counted the silver that was found in the house of Yahweh;
text.KJV: 12:10 And it was so when they saw that there was much money in the chest, that the kings scribe, and the high priest came vp, and they put vp in bags and told the money that was found in the house of the Lord.
text.NABRE: 12:10 Jehoiada the priest then took a chest, bored a hole in its lid, and set it beside the altar, on the right as one entered the house of the LORD. The priests who kept the doors would put into it all the silver that was brought to the house of the LORD.

text.NRSV: They would give the money that was weighed out into the hands of the workers who had the oversight of the house of the Lord; then they paid it out to the carpenters and the builders who worked on the house of the Lord,
text.Rotherham: then used they to give the silver that had been weighed out, into the hands of the doers of the work, who had oversight of the house of Yahweh,—and they brought it forth, to the carpenters, and to the builders, who were working upon the house of Yahweh;
text.KJV: 12:11 And they gaue the money, being told, into the handes of them that did the worke, that had the ouersight of the house of the Lord: and they laid it out to the carpenters and builders, that wrought vpon the house of the Lord,
text.NABRE: 12:11 When they noticed that there was a large amount of silver in the chest, the royal scribe would come up with the high priest, and they would gather up and weigh all the silver that was in the house of the LORD.

text.NRSV: to the masons and the stonecutters, as well as to buy timber and quarried stone for making the repairs on the house of the Lord, and for any outlay for repairs of the house.
text.Rotherham: and to the masons, and to the hewers of stone, and to buy timber, and hewn stone, for repairing the breaches of the house of Yahweh,—and to every one that went out upon the house, to repair it.
text.KJV: 12:12 And to Masons, and hewers of stone, and to buy timber, and hewed stone to repaire the breaches of the house of the Lord, and for all that was laid out for the house to repaire it.
text.NABRE: 12:12 The amount thus realized they turned over to the workers assigned to the house of the LORD. They in turn would pay it to the carpenters and builders working in the house of the LORD,

text.NRSV: But for the house of the Lord no basins of silver, snuffers, bowls, trumpets, or any vessels of gold, or of silver, were made from the money that was brought into the house of the Lord,
text.Rotherham: Howbeit there were not made for the house of Yahweh, bowls of silver, snuffers, dashing basins, trumpets, any vessel of gold, or any vessel of silver,—out of the silver that was brought into the house of Yahweh;
text.KJV: 12:13 Howbeit, there were not made for the house of the Lord, bowles of siluer, snuffers, basons, trumpets, any vessels of gold, or vessels of siluer, of the money that was brought into the house of the Lord:
text.NABRE: 12:13 and to the masons and stone cutters, and for the purchase of the wood and hewn stone used in repairing the breaches, and for any other expenses that were necessary to repair the house of the LORD.

text.NRSV: for that was given to the workers who were repairing the house of the Lord with it.
text.Rotherham: for, to the doers of the work, used they to give it; and so they repaired, therewith, the house of Yahweh.
text.KJV: 12:14 But they gaue that to the workemen, and repaired therewith the house of the Lord.
text.NABRE: 12:14 None of the valuables brought to the house of the LORD were used there to make silver basins, snuffers, bowls, trumpets, or any gold or silver article.

text.NRSV: They did not ask an accounting from those into whose hand they delivered the money to pay out to the workers, for they dealt honestly.
