text.Rotherham: And they used not to reckon with the men into whose hands they gave the silver, to give it to the doers of the work, because, with faithfulness, were, they, dealing.
text.KJV: 12:15 Moreouer, they reckned not with the men, into whose hand they deliuered the money to be bestowed on workmen: for they dealt faithfully.
text.NABRE: 12:15 Instead, they were given to the workers, and with them they repaired the house of the LORD.

text.NRSV: The money from the guilt offerings and the money from the sin offerings was not brought into the house of the Lord; it belonged to the priests.
text.Rotherham: Silver for guilt-offerings and silver for sin-offerings, was not brought into the house of Yahweh,—to the priests, they belonged.
text.KJV: 12:16 The trespasse money, and sinnemoney was not brought into the house of the Lord: it was the Priests.
text.NABRE: 12:16 Moreover, no reckoning was asked of those who were provided with the funds to give to the workers, because they held positions of trust.

text.NRSV: At that time King Hazael of Aram went up, fought against Gath, and took it. Then Hazael set his face to go up against Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: Then, came up Hazael, king of Syria, and fought against Gath, and captured it,—so Hazael set his face to go up against Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 12:17 ¶ Then Hazael king of Syria went vp, and fought against Gath, and tooke it: and Hazael set his face to goe vp to Ierusalem.
text.NABRE: 12:17 The funds from reparation offerings and from purification offerings, however, were not brought to the house of the LORD; they belonged to the priests.

text.NRSV: King Jehoash of Judah took all the votive gifts that his ancestors Jehoshaphat, Jehoram, and Ahaziah, the kings of Judah, had dedicated, and his own votive gifts, and all the gold that was found in the treasuries of the house of the Lord and of the king’s house, and sent these to King Hazael of Aram. Then Hazael withdrew from Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: Therefore did Jehoash, king of Judah, take all the hallowed things which Jehoshaphat and Jehoram and Ahaziah, his fathers, kings of Judah, had hallowed, and his own hallowed things, and all the gold that was found in the treasuries of the house of Yahweh and the house of the king,—and sent to Hazael king of Syria, so he went up from against Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 12:18 And Iehoash king of Iudah tooke all the hallowed things that Iehoshaphat, and Iehoram, and Ahaziah his fathers, kings of Iudah had dedicate, and his owne hallowed things, and all the gold that was found in the treasures of the house of the Lord, and in the kings house, and sent it to Hazael king of Syria, and hee went away from Ierusalem.
text.NABRE: 12:18 Then Hazael, king of Aram, came up and attacked Gath. When he had taken it, Hazael resolved to go on to attack Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: Now the rest of the acts of Joash, and all that he did, are they not written in the Book of the Annals of the Kings of Judah?
text.Rotherham: Now, the rest of the story of Joash, and all that he did, are, they, not written in the book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Judah?
text.KJV: 12:19 ¶ And the rest of the actes of Iehoash, and all that he did, are they not written in the booke of the Chronicles of the kings of Iudah?
text.NABRE: 12:19 Joash, king of Judah, took all the sacred offerings presented by his forebears, Jehoshaphat, Jehoram, and Ahaziah, kings of Judah, as well as his own, and all the gold there was in the treasuries of the house of the LORD and the king’s house, and sent them to King Hazael of Aram, who then turned away from Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: His servants arose, devised a conspiracy, and killed Joash in the house of Millo, on the way that goes down to Silla.
text.Rotherham: And his servants arose and made a conspiracy, and smote Joash, in the house of Millo which teeth down to Silla.
text.KJV: 12:20 And his seruants arose, and made a conspiracie, and slew Iehoash in the house of Millo, which goeth downe to Silla.
text.NABRE: 12:20 The rest of the acts of Joash, with all that he did, are recorded in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah.

text.NRSV: It was Jozacar son of Shimeath and Jehozabad son of Shomer, his servants, who struck him down, so that he died. He was buried with his ancestors in the city of David; then his son Amaziah succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: Yea, Jozabar son of Shimeath and Jehozabad son of Shomer, his servants, smote him, that he died, and he was buried with his fathers, in the city of David,—and, Amaziah his son, reigned in his stead.
text.KJV: 12:21 For Iozachar the sonne of Shimeath, and Iehozabad the sonne of Shomer, his seruants, smote him, and he died; and they buried him with his fathers in the citie of Dauid, and Amaziah his sonne reigned in his stead.
text.NABRE: 12:21 Certain of his officials entered into a conspiracy and struck Joash down at Beth-millo.

text.NRSV: In the twenty-third year of King Joash son of Ahaziah of Judah, Jehoahaz son of Jehu began to reign over Israel in Samaria; he reigned seventeen years.
text.Rotherham: In the twenty-third year of Joash son of Ahaziah king of Judah, began Jehoahaz, son of Jehu, to reign over Israel, in Samaria, [and he reigned] seventeen years.
text.KJV: 13:1 In the three and twentieth yeere of Ioash the sonne of Ahaziah king of Iudah, Iehoahaz the sonne of Iehu beganne to reigne ouer Israel in Samaria, and reigned seuenteene yeeres.
text.NABRE: 13:1 Chapter 13 - Reign of Jehoahaz of Israel. In the twenty-third year of Joash, son of Ahaziah, king of Judah, Jehoahaz, son of Jehu, became king over Israel in Samaria for seventeen years.

text.NRSV: He did what was evil in the sight of the Lord, and followed the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, which he caused Israel to commit; he did not depart from them.
text.Rotherham: And he did the thing that was wicked in the eyes of Yahweh,—and went after the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat which he caused, Israel, to commit, he departed not therefrom.
text.KJV: 13:2 And hee did that which was euill in the sight of the Lord, and followed the sinnes of Ieroboam the sonne of Nebat, which made Israel to sinne, he departed not there from.
text.NABRE: 13:2 He did what was evil in the LORD’s sight: he did not depart from following the sins that Jeroboam, son of Nebat, had caused Israel to commit.

text.NRSV: The anger of the Lord was kindled against Israel, so that he gave them repeatedly into the hand of King Hazael of Aram, then into the hand of Ben-hadad son of Hazael.
text.Rotherham: Then was kindled the anger of Yahweh against Israel—and he delivered them into the hand of Hazael king of Syria, and into the hand of Ben-hadad son of Hazael, continually.
text.KJV: 13:3 ¶ And the anger of the Lord was kindled against Israel, and hee deliuered them into the hand of Hazael king of Syria, and into the hand of Benhadad the sonne of Hazael, all their dayes.
text.NABRE: 13:3 The LORD was angry with Israel and for a long time gave them into the power of Hazael, king of Aram, and of Ben-hadad, son of Hazael.

text.NRSV: Then Jehoahaz entreated the Lord, and the Lord heeded him; for he saw the oppression of Israel, how the king of Aram oppressed them.
text.Rotherham: And Jehoahaz appeased the face of Yahweh,—and Yahweh hearkened unto him, because he had seen the oppression of Israel, for that, the king of Syria, oppressed them.
text.KJV: 13:4 And Iehoahaz besought the Lord, and the Lord hearkened vnto him: for hee saw the oppression of Israel, because the king of Syria oppressed them.
text.NABRE: 13:4 Then Jehoahaz entreated the LORD, who heard him, since he saw the oppression to which the king of Aram had subjected Israel.

text.NRSV: Therefore the Lord gave Israel a savior, so that they escaped from the hand of the Arameans; and the people of Israel lived in their homes as formerly.
text.Rotherham: So Yahweh gave unto Israel a saviour, and they went forth from under the hand of Syria,—and the sons of Israel dwelt in their own homes, as aforetime.
text.KJV: 13:5 (And the Lord gaue Israel a sauiour, so that they went out from vnder the hand of the Syrians: and the children of Israel dwelt in their tents as before-time.
text.NABRE: 13:5 So the LORD gave Israel a savior, and the Israelites, freed from the power of Aram, dwelt in their own tents as formerly.

text.NRSV: Nevertheless they did not depart from the sins of the house of Jeroboam, which he caused Israel to commit, but walked in them; the sacred pole also remained in Samaria.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit they departed not from the sins of the house of Jeroboam which he caused, Israel, to commit, therein, they walked,—moreover also, the Sacred Stem, still stood in Samaria.
text.KJV: 13:6 Neuerthelesse, they departed not from the sinnes of the house of Ieroboam, who made Israel sinne, but walked therein: and there remained the groue also in Samaria.)
text.NABRE: 13:6 Nevertheless, they did not desist from the sins the house of Jeroboam had caused Israel to commit, but persisted in them. The Asherah remained even in Samaria.

text.NRSV: For Jehoahaz was left with an army of not more than fifty horsemen, ten chariots and ten thousand footmen; for the king of Aram had destroyed them and made them like the dust at threshing.
text.Rotherham: For he had not left remaining unto Jehoahaz a people, save only fifty horsemen, and ten chariots, and ten thousand footmen,—for the king of Syria had destroyed them, and had made them like dust in threshing.
text.KJV: 13:7 Neither did he leaue of the people to Iehoahaz, but fiftie horsemen, and tenne charets, and tenne thousand footmen: for the king of Syria had destroyed them, and had made them like the dust by threshing.
text.NABRE: 13:7 No army was left to Jehoahaz, except fifty horses with ten chariots and ten thousand foot soldiers, since the king of Aram had destroyed them and trampled them like dust.

text.NRSV: Now the rest of the acts of Jehoahaz and all that he did, and his might, are they not written in the Book of the Annals of the Kings of Israel?
text.Rotherham: Now, the rest of the story of Jehoahaz, and all that he did, and his might, are, they, not written in the book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Israel?
text.KJV: 13:8 ¶ Nowe the rest of the actes of Iehoahaz, and all that he did, and his might, are they not written in the booke of the Chronicles of the kings of Israel?
text.NABRE: 13:8 The rest of the acts of Jehoahaz, with all that he did and his valor, are recorded in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel.

text.NRSV: So Jehoahaz slept with his ancestors, and they buried him in Samaria; then his son Joash succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: So then Jehoahaz slept with his fathers, and they buried him in Samaria,—and, Joash his son, reigned, in his stead.
text.KJV: 13:9 And Iehoahaz slept with his fathers, and they buried him in Samaria, and Ioash his sonne reigned in his stead.
text.NABRE: 13:9 Jehoahaz rested with his ancestors; he was buried in Samaria and his son Joash succeeded him as king.

text.NRSV: In the thirty-seventh year of King Joash of Judah, Jehoash son of Jehoahaz began to reign over Israel in Samaria; he reigned sixteen years.
text.Rotherham: In the thirty-seventh year of Joash king of Judah, began Jehoash son of Jehoahaz to reign over Israel, in Samaria, [and he reigned] sixteen years.
text.KJV: 13:10 ¶ In the thirty and seuenth yeere of Ioash king of Iudah, beganne Iehoash the sonne of Iehoahaz to reigne ouer Israel in Samaria, and reigned sixteene yeeres.
text.NABRE: 13:10 Reign of Joash of Israel. In the thirty-seventh year of Joash, king of Judah, Joash, son of Jehoahaz, became king over Israel in Samaria sixteen years.

text.NRSV: He also did what was evil in the sight of the Lord; he did not depart from all the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, which he caused Israel to commit, but he walked in them.
text.Rotherham: And he did that which was wicked in the eyes of Yahweh,—he turned not away from all the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, which he caused, Israel, to commit, therein, he walked.
text.KJV: 13:11 And hee did that which was euill in the sight of the Lord; hee departed not from all the sinnes of Ieroboam the sonne of Nebat, who made Israel sinne: but hee walked therein.
text.NABRE: 13:11 He did what was evil in the LORD’s sight; he did not desist from any of the sins Jeroboam, son of Nebat, had caused Israel to commit, but persisted in them.

text.NRSV: Now the rest of the acts of Joash, and all that he did, and the might with which he fought against King Amaziah of Judah, are they not written in the Book of the Annals of the Kings of Israel?
text.Rotherham: Now, the rest of the story of Joash, and all that he did, and his might, wherewith he fought against Amaziah king of Judah, are, they, not written in the book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Israel?
text.KJV: 13:12 And the rest of the actes of Ioash, and all that hee did, and his might, wherewith hee fought against Amaziah king of Iudah, are they not written in the booke of the chronicles of the kings of Israel?
text.NABRE: 13:12 The rest of the acts of Joash, with all that he did and his valor, and how he fought with Amaziah, king of Judah, are recorded in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel.

text.NRSV: So Joash slept with his ancestors, and Jeroboam sat upon his throne; Joash was buried in Samaria with the kings of Israel.
text.Rotherham: So then Joash slept with his fathers, and, Jeroboam, took his seat on his throne,—and Joash was buried in Samaria, with the kings of Israel.
text.KJV: 13:13 And Ioash slept with his fathers, and Ieroboam sate vpon his throne: and Ioash was buried in Samaria with the kings of Israel.
text.NABRE: 13:13 Joash rested with his ancestors. Then Jeroboam sat on his throne. Joash was buried in Samaria with the kings of Israel.

text.NRSV: Now when Elisha had fallen sick with the illness of which he was to die, King Joash of Israel went down to him, and wept before him, crying, “My father, my father! The chariots of Israel and its horsemen!”
text.Rotherham: Now, Elisha, had fallen sick of his sickness whereof he was about to die,—so then Joash king of Israel came down unto him, and wept upon his face, and said, My father! my father! The chariots of Israel and the horsemen thereof!
text.KJV: 13:14 ¶ Nowe Elisha was fallen sicke, of his sicknesse whereof he died, and Ioash the king of Israel came downe vnto him, and wept ouer his face, and said, O my father, my father, the charet of Israel, and the horsemen thereof.
text.NABRE: 13:14 Elisha’s Deathbed Prophecy. When Elisha was suffering from the sickness of which he was to die, Joash, king of Israel, went down to weep over him. “My father, my father!” he exclaimed, “Israel’s chariot and steeds!”

text.NRSV: Elisha said to him, “Take a bow and arrows”; so he took a bow and arrows.
text.Rotherham: And Elisha said unto him, Take bow and arrows. So he took unto him bow and arrows.
text.KJV: 13:15 And Elisha said vnto him, Take bowe and arrowes. And he tooke vnto him bowe and arrowes.
text.NABRE: 13:15 Elisha said to him, “Take bow and arrows,” and he took bow and arrows.

text.NRSV: Then he said to the king of Israel, “Draw the bow”; and he drew it. Elisha laid his hands on the king’s hands.
text.Rotherham: Then said he unto the king of Israel, Let thy hand rest upon the bow. So he let his hand rest thereon. Then Elisha put his own hands upon the hands of the king;
text.KJV: 13:16 And he said to the king of Israel, Put thine hand vpon the bowe. And he put his hand vpon it: and Elisha put his hands vpon the kings hands.
text.NABRE: 13:16 Elisha said to the king of Israel, “Rest your hand on the bow,” and he rested his hand on it. Elisha placed his hands over the king’s hands

text.NRSV: Then he said, “Open the window eastward”; and he opened it. Elisha said, “Shoot”; and he shot. Then he said, “The Lord’s arrow of victory, the arrow of victory over Aram! For you shall fight the Arameans in Aphek until you have made an end of them.”
text.Rotherham: and said—Open the lattice eastward. And he opened it. Then said Elisha—Shoot! And he shot. Then he said,—The arrow of victory by Yahweh, yea the arrow of victory over Syria, therefore shalt thou smite Syria in Aphek, till it be consumed.
text.KJV: 13:17 And he sayd, Open the window Eastward. And hee opened it. Then Elisha sayd, Shoote. And he shot. And he said; The arrowe of the Lords deliuerance, and the arrowe of deliuerance from Syria: for thou shalt smite the Syrians in Aphek, till thou haue consumed them.
text.NABRE: 13:17 and said, “Open the window toward the east.” He opened it. Elisha said, “Shoot,” and he shot. He said, “An arrow of victory for the LORD! An arrow of victory over Aram! You will beat Aram at Aphek and finish him!”

text.NRSV: He continued, “Take the arrows”; and he took them. He said to the king of Israel, “Strike the ground with them”; he struck three times, and stopped.
text.Rotherham: And he said—Take the arrows. So he took them. Then said he unto the king of Israel—Smite unto the ground. So he smote three times, and then stayed.
text.KJV: 13:18 And he sayd, Take the arrowes. And he tooke them. And hee said vnto the king of Israel, Smite vpon the ground. And he smote thrise, and stayed.
text.NABRE: 13:18 Then he said to the king of Israel, “Take the arrows,” which he did. Elisha said to the king of Israel, “Beat the ground!” He beat the ground three times and stopped.

text.NRSV: Then the man of God was angry with him, and said, “You should have struck five or six times; then you would have struck down Aram until you had made an end of it, but now you will strike down Aram only three times.”
text.Rotherham: Then was the man of God wroth against him, and said—Thou shouldest have smitten five or six times, then, hadst thou smitten Syria, until it had been consumed; But, now, three times, shalt thou smite Syria.
text.KJV: 13:19 And the man of God was wroth with him, and saide, Thou shouldest haue smitten fiue or sixe times, then haddest thou smitten Syria till thou haddest consumed it: whereas now thou shalt smite Syria but thrice.
text.NABRE: 13:19 The man of God became angry with him and said, “You should have beat five or six times. You would have beaten Aram and finished him. Now you will beat Aram only three times.”

text.NRSV: So Elisha died, and they buried him. Now bands of Moabites used to invade the land in the spring of the year.
text.Rotherham: And Elisha died, and they buried him,—now, troops of Moabites, used to enter the land, at the coming in of the year;
text.KJV: 13:20 ¶ And Elisha died, and they buried him: And the bands of the Moabites inuaded the land at the comming in of the yeere.
text.NABRE: 13:20 And so Elisha died and was buried. At that time of year, bands of Moabites used to raid the land.

text.NRSV: As a man was being buried, a marauding band was seen and the man was thrown into the grave of Elisha; as soon as the man touched the bones of Elisha, he came to life and stood on his feet.
text.Rotherham: and it came to pass, as, they, were burying a man, that, lo! they saw a troop, so they cast the man into the sepulchre of Elisha,—and, as soon as the man touched the bones of Elisha, he came to life again, and rose up on his feet.
text.KJV: 13:21 And it came to passe as they were burying a man, that behold, they spyed a band of men, and they cast the man into the sepulchre of Elisha: and when the man was let downe, and touched the bones of Elisha, he reuiued, and stood vp on his feete.
text.NABRE: 13:21 Once some people were burying a man, when suddenly they saw such a raiding band. So they cast the man into the grave of Elisha, and everyone went off. But when the man came in contact with the bones of Elisha, he came back to life and got to his feet.

text.NRSV: Now King Hazael of Aram oppressed Israel all the days of Jehoahaz.
text.Rotherham: And, Hazael king of Syria, had oppressed Israel all the days of Jehoahaz.
text.KJV: 13:22 ¶ But Hazael king of Syria, oppressed Israel all the dayes of Iehoahaz.
text.NABRE: 13:22 King Hazael of Aram oppressed Israel all the days of Jehoahaz.

text.NRSV: But the Lord was gracious to them and had compassion on them; he turned toward them, because of his covenant with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, and would not destroy them; nor has he banished them from his presence until now.
text.Rotherham: Then was Yahweh gracious unto them, and had compassion upon them, and turned unto them, for the sake of his covenant with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob,—and was not willing to destroy them, nor to cast them off from his presence, as yet.
text.KJV: 13:23 And the Lord was gracious vnto them, and had compassion on them, and had respect vnto them, because of his couenant with Abraham, Isaac, and Iacob, and would not destroy them, neither cast hee them from his presence as yet.
text.NABRE: 13:23 But the LORD was gracious with Israel and looked on them with compassion because of his covenant with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. He was unwilling to destroy them or to cast them out from his presence even up to now.

text.NRSV: When King Hazael of Aram died, his son Ben-hadad succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: So then Hazael king of Syria died; and, Ben-hadad his son, reigned, in his stead.
text.KJV: 13:24 So Hazael the king of Syria dyed, and Benhadad his sonne reigned in his stead.
text.NABRE: 13:24 So when King Hazael of Aram died and his son Ben-hadad succeeded him as king,

text.NRSV: Then Jehoash son of Jehoahaz took again from Ben-hadad son of Hazael the towns that he had taken from his father Jehoahaz in war. Three times Joash defeated him and recovered the towns of Israel.
text.Rotherham: And Jehoash son of Jehoahaz again took the cities out of the hand of Ben-hadad son of Hazael, which he had taken out of the hand of Jehoahaz his father, in war,—three times, did Joash smite him, and recover the cities of Israel.
text.KJV: 13:25 And Iehoash the sonne of Iehoahaz tooke againe out of the hand of Benhadad the sonne of Hazael, the cities which he had taken out of the hand of Iehoahaz his father, by warre: three times did Ioash beat him, and recouered the cities of Israel.
text.NABRE: 13:25 Joash, son of Jehoahaz, took back from Ben-hadad, son of Hazael, the cities Hazael had taken in battle from Jehoahaz, his father. Three times Joash beat him, and thus recovered the cities of Israel.

text.NRSV: In the second year of King Joash son of Joahaz of Israel, King Amaziah son of Joash of Judah began to reign.
text.Rotherham: In the second year of Joash son of Joahaz king of Israel, began Amaziah son of Joash king of Judah to reign.
text.KJV: 14:1 In the second yeere of Ioash sonne of Iehoahaz king of Israel, reigned Amaziah the sonne of Ioash king of Iudah.
text.NABRE: 14:1 Chapter 14 - Reign of Amaziah of Judah. In the second year of Joash, son of Jehoahaz, king of Israel, Amaziah, son of Joash, king of Judah, became king.

text.NRSV: He was twenty-five years old when he began to reign, and he reigned twenty-nine years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Jehoaddin of Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: Twenty-five years old, was he when he began to reign, and twenty-nine years, reigned he in Jerusalem,—and, his mother’s name, was Jehoaddan of Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 14:2 Hee was twentie and fiue yeeres olde when he began to reigne, and reigned twentie and nine yeeres in Ierusalem: and his mothers name was Iehoaddan of Ierusalem.
text.NABRE: 14:2 He was twenty-five years old when he became king, and he reigned twenty-nine years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Jehoaddin, from Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: He did what was right in the sight of the Lord, yet not like his ancestor David; in all things he did as his father Joash had done.
text.Rotherham: And he did that which was right in the eyes of Yahweh, only, not like David his father: according to all that Joash his father did, so he did;
text.KJV: 14:3 And he did that which was right in the sight of the Lord, yet not like Dauid his father: hee did according to all things as Ioash his father did.
text.NABRE: 14:3 He did what was right in the LORD’s eyes, though not like David his father. He did just as his father Joash had done,

text.NRSV: But the high places were not removed; the people still sacrificed and made offerings on the high places.
text.Rotherham: only, the high places, took they not away,—still were the people sacrificing and burning incense in the high places.
text.KJV: 14:4 Howbeit, the high places were not taken away: as yet the people did sacrifice, and burnt incense on the high places.
text.NABRE: 14:4 though the high places did not disappear, and the people continued to sacrifice and to burn incense on the high places.

text.NRSV: As soon as the royal power was firmly in his hand, he killed his servants who had murdered his father the king.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that, as soon as the kingdom was confirmed in his hand, he smote his servants who smote the king his father;
text.KJV: 14:5 ¶ And it came to passe assoone as the kingdome was confirmed in his hand, that he slew his seruants which had slaine the king his father.
text.NABRE: 14:5 When Amaziah had the kingdom firmly in hand, he struck down the officials who had struck down the king, his father.

text.NRSV: But he did not put to death the children of the murderers; according to what is written in the book of the law of Moses, where the Lord commanded, “Parents shall not be put to death for the children, nor shall children be put to death for the parents; but all shall be put to death for their own sins.”
text.Rotherham: but, the sons of them that smote him, slew he not,—as it is written in the book of the law of Moses, which Yahweh commanded, saying—Fathers, shall not be put to death, for sons, nor shall, sons, be put to death, for fathers, but every man, for his own sin, shall be put to death.
text.KJV: 14:6 But the children of the murderers he slew not, according vnto that which is written in the booke of the Law of Moses, wherein the Lord commanded, saying, The fathers shal not be put to death for the children, nor the children be put to death for the fathers: but euery man shall be put to death for his owne sinne.
text.NABRE: 14:6 But their children he did not put to death, according to what is written in the book of the law of Moses, which the LORD commanded: “Parents shall not be put to death for their children, nor shall children be put to death for their parents; only for one’s own crimes shall a person be put to death.”

text.NRSV: He killed ten thousand Edomites in the Valley of Salt and took Sela by storm; he called it Joktheel, which is its name to this day.
text.Rotherham: He, smote Edom, in the valley of salt, ten thousand, and seized Sela, in the war,—and called the name thereof Joktheel, [as it is] until this day.
text.KJV: 14:7 He slew of Edom in the valley of salt, ten thousand, and tooke Selah by warre, and called the name of it, Ioktheel, vnto this day.
text.NABRE: 14:7 Amaziah struck down ten thousand Edomites in the Salt Valley. He took Sela in battle and renamed it Joktheel, the name it has to this day.

text.NRSV: Then Amaziah sent messengers to King Jehoash son of Jehoahaz son of Jehu of Israel, saying, “Come, let us look one another in the face.”
text.Rotherham: Then, sent Amaziah messengers unto Jehoash son of Jehoahaz son of Jehu, king of Israel, saying,—Come now, let us look one another in the face.
text.KJV: 14:8 ¶ Then Amaziah sent messengers to Iehoash the sonne of Iehoahaz sonne of Iehu king of Israel, saying, Come, let vs looke one another in the face.
text.NABRE: 14:8 Then Amaziah sent messengers to Joash, son of Jehoahaz, son of Jehu, king of Israel, with this message: “Come, let us meet face to face.”

text.NRSV: King Jehoash of Israel sent word to King Amaziah of Judah, “A thornbush on Lebanon sent to a cedar on Lebanon, saying, ‘Give your daughter to my son for a wife’; but a wild animal of Lebanon passed by and trampled down the thornbush.
text.Rotherham: And Jehoash king of Israel sent unto Amaziah king of Judah, saying, A thistle that was in Lebanon, sent unto a cedar that was in Lebanon, saying—Give thy daughter to my son to wife,—and there passed by a beast of the field that was in Lebanon, and trampled down the thistle:
text.KJV: 14:9 And Iehoash the king of Israel sent to Amaziah king of Iudah, saying, The thistle that was in Lebanon, sent to the Cedar that was in Lebanon, saying, Giue thy daughter to my sonne to wife. And there passed by a wilde beast that was in Lebanon, and trode downe the thistle.
text.NABRE: 14:9 Joash, king of Israel, sent this reply to Amaziah, king of Judah: “A thistle of Lebanon sent word to a cedar of Lebanon, ‘Give your daughter to my son in marriage,’ but an animal of Lebanon passed by and trampled the thistle underfoot.

text.NRSV: You have indeed defeated Edom, and your heart has lifted you up. Be content with your glory, and stay at home; for why should you provoke trouble so that you fall, you and Judah with you?”
text.Rotherham: Thou hast, smitten, Edom, and, thy heart, would lift thee up,—Glory, and stay at home! Wherefore, then, shouldest thou contend with misfortune, and fall, thou, and Judah with thee?
text.KJV: 14:10 Thou hast indeed smitten Edom, and thine heart hath lifted thee vp: glory of this, and tary at home: for why shouldest thou meddle to thy hurt, that thou shouldest fall, euen thou, and Iudah with thee?
text.NABRE: 14:10 You have indeed struck down Edom, and your heart is lifted up; enjoy your glory, but stay home! Why bring misfortune and failure on yourself and on Judah with you?”

text.NRSV: But Amaziah would not listen. So King Jehoash of Israel went up; he and King Amaziah of Judah faced one another in battle at Beth-shemesh, which belongs to Judah.
text.Rotherham: But Amaziah hearkened not. So then Jehoash king of Israel came up, and they looked one another in the face, he and Amaziah king of Judah,—in Beth-shemesh, which belongeth unto Judah.
text.KJV: 14:11 But Amaziah would not heare: therefore Iehoash king of Israel went vp, and hee, and Amaziah king of Iudah, looked one another in the face at Bethshemesh, which belongeth to Iudah.
text.NABRE: 14:11 But Amaziah did not listen. So Joash, king of Israel, advanced, and he and Amaziah, king of Judah, met face to face at Beth-shemesh of Judah,

text.NRSV: Judah was defeated by Israel; everyone fled home.
text.Rotherham: Then was Judah defeated before Israel,—and they fled, every man to his own home;
text.KJV: 14:12 And Iudah was put to the worse before Israel, and they fled euery man to their tents.
text.NABRE: 14:12 and Judah was defeated by Israel, and all fled to their tents.

text.NRSV: King Jehoash of Israel captured King Amaziah of Judah son of Jehoash son of Ahaziah at Beth-shemesh; he came to Jerusalem, and broke down the wall of Jerusalem from the Ephraim Gate to the Corner Gate, a distance of four hundred cubits.
text.Rotherham: and, upon Amaziah, king of Judah, son of Jehoash son of Ahaziah, did Jehoash king of Israel, seize, in Beth-shemesh,—and entered Jerusalem, and brake down the wall of Jerusalem, at the gate of Ephraim, as far as the corner gate, four hundred cubits;
text.KJV: 14:13 And Iehoash king of Israel tooke Amaziah king of Iudah, the sonne of Iehoash the sonne of Ahaziah at Bethshemesh, and came to Ierusalem, and brake downe the wall of Ierusalem, from the gate of Ephraim, vnto the corner gate, foure hundred cubites.
text.NABRE: 14:13 But Amaziah, king of Judah, son of Joash, son of Ahaziah, was captured by Joash, king of Israel, at Beth-shemesh. When they came to Jerusalem Joash tore down the wall of Jerusalem, from the Gate of Ephraim to the Corner Gate, four hundred cubits.

text.NRSV: He seized all the gold and silver, and all the vessels that were found in the house of the Lord and in the treasuries of the king’s house, as well as hostages; then he returned to Samaria.
text.Rotherham: and took all the gold and the silver and all the vessels that were found in the house of Yahweh, and in the treasuries of the house of the king, and hostages,—and returned to Samaria.
text.KJV: 14:14 And he tooke all the golde and siluer, and all the vessels that were found in the house of the Lord, and in the treasures of the kings house, and hostages, and returned to Samaria.
text.NABRE: 14:14 He took all the gold and silver and all the vessels found in the house of the LORD and in the treasuries of the king’s house, and hostages as well. Then he returned to Samaria.

text.NRSV: Now the rest of the acts that Jehoash did, his might, and how he fought with King Amaziah of Judah, are they not written in the Book of the Annals of the Kings of Israel?
text.Rotherham: Now, the rest of the story of Jehoash, what he did, and his might and how he fought with Amaziah king of Judah, are, they, not written in the book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Israel?
text.KJV: 14:15 ¶ Now the rest of the acts of Iehoash which he did, and his might, and how he fought with Amaziah king of Iudah, are they not written in the booke of the Chronicles of the kings of Israel?
text.NABRE: 14:15 The rest of the acts of Joash, what he did and his valor, and how he made war against Amaziah, king of Judah, are recorded in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel.

text.NRSV: Jehoash slept with his ancestors, and was buried in Samaria with the kings of Israel; then his son Jeroboam succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: And Jehoash slept with his fathers, and was buried in Samaria, with the kings of Israel,—and, Jeroboam his son, reigned, in his stead.
text.KJV: 14:16 And Iehoash slept with his fathers, and was buried in Samaria, with the kings of Israel, and Ieroboam his sonne reigned in his stead.
text.NABRE: 14:16 Joash rested with his ancestors; he was buried in Samaria with the kings of Israel, and his son Jeroboam succeeded him as king.

text.NRSV: King Amaziah son of Joash of Judah lived fifteen years after the death of King Jehoash son of Jehoahaz of Israel.
text.Rotherham: And Amaziah son of Joash king of Judah, lived, after the death of Jehoash son of Jehoahaz king of Israel, fifteen years.
text.KJV: 14:17 ¶ And Amaziah the sonne of Ioash king of Iudah, liued after the death of Iehoash sonne of Iehoahaz king of Israel, fifteene yeeres.
text.NABRE: 14:17 Amaziah, son of Joash, king of Judah, survived Joash, son of Jehoahaz, king of Israel, by fifteen years.

text.NRSV: Now the rest of the acts of Amaziah, are they not written in the Book of the Annals of the Kings of Judah?
text.Rotherham: Now, the rest of the story of Amaziah, is, it, not written in the book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Judah?
text.KJV: 14:18 And the rest of the acts of Amaziah, are they not written in the booke of the Chronicles of the kings of Iudah?
text.NABRE: 14:18 The rest of the acts of Amaziah are recorded in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah.

text.NRSV: A conspiracy was formed against him in Jerusalem, and he fled to Lachish. But they sent after him to Lachish, and killed him there.
text.Rotherham: And, when they made against him a conspiracy in Jerusalem, he fled to Lachish,—but they sent after him to Lachish, and slew him, there.
text.KJV: 14:19 Now they made a conspiracie against him in Ierusalem: and he fled to Lachish, but they sent after him to Lachish, and slew him there.
text.NABRE: 14:19 When a conspiracy was formed against him in Jerusalem, he fled to Lachish. But he was pursued to Lachish and killed there.

text.NRSV: They brought him on horses; he was buried in Jerusalem with his ancestors in the city of David.
text.Rotherham: And they bare him on horses,—and he was buried in Jerusalem, with his fathers, in the city of David.
text.KJV: 14:20 And they brought him on horses, and he was buried at Ierusalem with his fathers, in the city of Dauid.
text.NABRE: 14:20 He was brought back on horses and was buried in Jerusalem with his ancestors in the City of David.

text.NRSV: All the people of Judah took Azariah, who was sixteen years old, and made him king to succeed his father Amaziah.
text.Rotherham: And all the people of Judah took Azariah, he, being sixteen yearn old,—and made, him, king instead of his father Amaziah.
text.KJV: 14:21 ¶ And all the people of Iudah tooke Azariah (which was sixteene yeeres old) and made him king in stead of his father Amaziah.
text.NABRE: 14:21 Thereupon all the people of Judah took Azariah, who was only sixteen years old, and made him king to succeed Amaziah, his father.

text.NRSV: He rebuilt Elath and restored it to Judah, after the king slept with his ancestors.
text.Rotherham: He, built Elath, and restored it to Judah,—after the king slept with his fathers.
text.KJV: 14:22 He built Elath, and restored it to Iudah, after that the king slept with his fathers.
text.NABRE: 14:22 It was he who rebuilt Elath and restored it to Judah, after the king rested with his ancestors.

text.NRSV: In the fifteenth year of King Amaziah son of Joash of Judah, King Jeroboam son of Joash of Israel began to reign in Samaria; he reigned forty-one years.
text.Rotherham: In the fifteenth year of Amaziah, son of Joash, king of Judah, began Jeroboam, son of Joash, king of Israel, to reign in Samaria, [and he reigned] forty-one years.
text.KJV: 14:23 ¶ In the fifteenth yeere of Amaziah the sonne of Ioash king of Iudah, Ieroboam the sonne of Ioash king of Israel began to raigne in Samaria, and raigned forty and one yeeres:
text.NABRE: 14:23 Reign of Jeroboam II of Israel. In the fifteenth year of Amaziah, son of Joash, king of Judah, Jeroboam, son of Joash, king of Israel, became king in Samaria for forty-one years.

text.NRSV: He did what was evil in the sight of the Lord; he did not depart from all the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, which he caused Israel to commit.
text.Rotherham: And he did the thing that was wicked in the eyes of Yahweh,—he turned not away from any of the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, which he caused, Israel, to commit.
text.KJV: 14:24 And hee did that which was euill in the sight of the Lord: hee departed not from all the sinnes of Ieroboam the sonne of Nebat, who made Israel to sinne.
text.NABRE: 14:24 He did evil in the LORD’s sight; he did not desist from any of the sins that Jeroboam, son of Nebat, had caused Israel to commit.

text.NRSV: He restored the border of Israel from Lebo-hamath as far as the Sea of the Arabah, according to the word of the Lord, the God of Israel, which he spoke by his servant Jonah son of Amittai, the prophet, who was from Gath-hepher.
text.Rotherham: He, restored the boundary of Israel, from the entering in of Hamath, unto the sea of the waste plain,—according to the word of Yahweh, God of Israel, which he spake by the hand of his servant Jonah, son of Amittai, the prophet, who was of Gath-hepher.
text.KJV: 14:25 Hee restored the coast of Israel, from the entring of Hamath, vnto the sea of the plaine, according to the word of the Lord God of Israel, which he spake by the hand of his seruant Ionah, the sonne of Amittai the Prophet, which was of Gath Hepher.
text.NABRE: 14:25 He restored the boundaries of Israel from Lebo-hamath to the sea of the Arabah, as the LORD, the God of Israel, had foretold through his servant, the prophet Jonah, son of Amittai, from Gath-hepher.

text.NRSV: For the Lord saw that the distress of Israel was very bitter; there was no one left, bond or free, and no one to help Israel.
text.Rotherham: For Yahweh saw the humiliation of Israel, that it was bitter, indeed,—and that there was no one shut up, nor any one left at large, no one indeed to help Israel.
text.KJV: 14:26 For the Lord saw the affliction of Israel, that it was very bitter: for there was not any shut vp, nor any left, nor any helper for Israel.
text.NABRE: 14:26 For the LORD saw the very bitter affliction of Israel, where there was neither bond nor free, no one at all to help Israel.

text.NRSV: But the Lord had not said that he would blot out the name of Israel from under heaven, so he saved them by the hand of Jeroboam son of Joash.
text.Rotherham: Neither had Yahweh spoken, to wipe out the name of Israel from under the heavens,—so he saved them, by the hand of Jeroboam son of Joash.
text.KJV: 14:27 And the Lord said not, that hee would blot out the name of Israel from vnder heauen: but he saued them by the hand of Ieroboam the sonne of Ioash.
text.NABRE: 14:27 Since the LORD had not resolved to wipe out the name of Israel from under the heavens, he saved them through Jeroboam, son of Joash.

text.NRSV: Now the rest of the acts of Jeroboam, and all that he did, and his might, how he fought, and how he recovered for Israel Damascus and Hamath, which had belonged to Judah, are they not written in the Book of the Annals of the Kings of Israel?
text.Rotherham: Now, the rest of the story of Jeroboam, and all that he did, and his might when he warred, and how he restored Damascus and Hamath to Judah in Israel, are, they, not written in the book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Israel?
text.KJV: 14:28 ¶ Now the rest of the actes of Ieroboam, and all that he did, and his might, how he warred, and how he recouered Damascus and Hamath, which belonged to Iudah, for Israel, are they not written in the booke of the Chronicles of the kings of Israel?
text.NABRE: 14:28 The rest of the acts of Jeroboam, with all that he did and his valor, how he fought, and how he regained Damascus and Hamath for Israel, are recorded in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel.

text.NRSV: Jeroboam slept with his ancestors, the kings of Israel; and his son Zechariah succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: And Jeroboam slept with his fathers, with the kings of Israel,—and, Zechariah his son, reigned, in his stead.
text.KJV: 14:29 And Ieroboam slept with his fathers, euen with the kings of Israel, and Zachariah his sonne reigned in his stead.
text.NABRE: 14:29 Jeroboam rested with his ancestors, the kings of Israel, and his son Zechariah succeeded him as king.

text.NRSV: In the twenty-seventh year of King Jeroboam of Israel, Azariah son of King Amaziah of Judah began to reign.
text.Rotherham: In the twenty-seventh year of Jeroboam king of Israel, began Azariah son of Amaziah king of Judah to reign.
text.KJV: 15:1 In the twenty and seuenth yeere of Ieroboam king of Israel, began Azariah sonne of Amaziah king of Iudah to reigne.
text.NABRE: 15:1 Chapter 15 - Reign of Azariah of Judah. In the twenty-seventh year of Jeroboam, king of Israel, Azariah, son of Amaziah, king of Judah, became king.

text.NRSV: He was sixteen years old when he began to reign, and he reigned fifty-two years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Jecoliah of Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: Sixteen years old, was he when he began to reign, and, fifty-two years, reigned he in Jerusalem,—and, the name of his mother, was Jecholiah, of Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 15:2 Sixteene yeeres old was he when he began to reigne, and he reigned two aud fifty yeeres in Ierusalem: and his mothers name was Iecholiah of Ierusalem.
text.NABRE: 15:2 He was sixteen years old when he became king, and he reigned fifty-two years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Jecholiah, from Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: He did what was right in the sight of the Lord, just as his father Amaziah had done.
text.Rotherham: And he did that which was right in the eyes of Yahweh,—according to all that, Amaziah his father, had done.
text.KJV: 15:3 And he did that which was right in the sight of the Lord, according to all that his father Amaziah had done;
text.NABRE: 15:3 He did what was right in the LORD’s sight, just as his father Amaziah had done,

text.NRSV: Nevertheless the high places were not removed; the people still sacrificed and made offerings on the high places.
text.Rotherham: Only, the high places, took they not away,—still were the people sacrificing and burning incense in the high places.
text.KJV: 15:4 Saue that the high places were not remoued: the people sacrificed, and burnt incense still on the high places.
text.NABRE: 15:4 though the high places did not disappear, and the people continued to sacrifice and to burn incense on the high places.

text.NRSV: The Lord struck the king, so that he was leprous to the day of his death, and lived in a separate house. Jotham the king’s son was in charge of the palace, governing the people of the land.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh smote the king, so that he became a leper, until the day of his death, and dwelt in a lazar-house,—and, Jotham, son of the king, was over the house, judging the people of the land.
text.KJV: 15:5 ¶ And the Lord smote the king, so that hee was a Leper vnto the day of his death, and dwelt in a seuerall house, and Iotham the kings sonne was ouer the house, iudging the people of the land.
text.NABRE: 15:5 The LORD afflicted the king, and he was a leper until the day he died. He lived in a house apart, while Jotham, the king’s son, was master of the palace and ruled the people of the land.

text.NRSV: Now the rest of the acts of Azariah, and all that he did, are they not written in the Book of the Annals of the Kings of Judah?
text.Rotherham: Now, the rest of the story of Azariah, and all that he did, are, they, not written in the book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Judah.
text.KJV: 15:6 And the rest of the actes of Azariah, and all that hee did, are they not written in the booke of the Chronicles of the kings of Iudah?
text.NABRE: 15:6 The rest of the acts of Azariah, and all that he did, are recorded in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah.

text.NRSV: Azariah slept with his ancestors, and they buried him with his ancestors in the city of David; his son Jotham succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: And Azariah slept with his fathers, and they buried him with his fathers, in the city of David,—and, Jotham his son, reigned, in his stead.
text.KJV: 15:7 So Azariah slept with his fathers, and they buried him with his fathers in the city of Dauid, and Iotham his sonne reigned in his stead.
text.NABRE: 15:7 Azariah rested with his ancestors, and was buried with them in the City of David, and his son Jotham succeeded him as king.

text.NRSV: In the thirty-eighth year of King Azariah of Judah, Zechariah son of Jeroboam reigned over Israel in Samaria six months.
text.Rotherham: In the thirty-eighth year of Azariah king of Judah, began Zechariah son of Jeroboam to reign over Israel, in Samaria, [and he reigned] six months.
text.KJV: 15:8 ¶ In the thirty and eight yeere of Azariah king of Iudah, did Zachariah the sonne of Ieroboam reigne ouer Israel in Samaria sixe moneths.
text.NABRE: 15:8 Reign of Zechariah of Israel. In the thirty-eighth year of Azariah, king of Judah, Zechariah, son of Jeroboam, became king over Israel in Samaria for six months.

text.NRSV: He did what was evil in the sight of the Lord, as his ancestors had done. He did not depart from the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, which he caused Israel to commit.
text.Rotherham: And he did the thing that was wicked in the eyes of Yahweh, as did his fathers,—he turned not away from the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, which he caused, Israel, to commit.
text.KJV: 15:9 And hee did that which was euil in the sight of the Lord, as his fathers had done: he departed not from the sinnes of Ieroboam the sonne of Nebat, who made Israel to sinne.
text.NABRE: 15:9 He did what was evil in the LORD’s sight, as his ancestors had done, and did not desist from the sins that Jeroboam, son of Nebat, had caused Israel to commit.

text.NRSV: Shallum son of Jabesh conspired against him, and struck him down in public and killed him, and succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: Then conspired against him, Shallum son of Jabesh, and smote him before the people, and slew him,—and reigned in his stead.
text.KJV: 15:10 And Shallum the sonne of Iabesh, conspired against him, and smote him before the people, and slewe him, and reigned in his stead.
text.NABRE: 15:10 Shallum, son of Jabesh, plotted against him and struck him down at Ibleam. He killed him and reigned in his place.

text.NRSV: Now the rest of the acts of Zechariah are written in the Book of the Annals of the Kings of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Now, the rest of the story of Zechariah, behold it! written in the book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Israel.
text.KJV: 15:11 And the rest of the actes of Zachariah, beholde, they are written in the booke of the chronicles of the kings of Israel.
text.NABRE: 15:11 As for the rest of the acts of Zechariah, these are recorded in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel.

text.NRSV: This was the promise of the Lord that he gave to Jehu, “Your sons shall sit on the throne of Israel to the fourth generation.” And so it happened.
text.Rotherham: That, was the word of Yahweh, which he spake unto Jehu, saying, Thy sons, to four generations, shall sit on the throne of Israel. And it was so!
text.KJV: 15:12 This was the word of the Lord which he spake vnto Iehu, saying, Thy sonnes shall sit on the throne of Israel, vnto the fourth generation. And so it came to passe.
text.NABRE: 15:12 This was the word the LORD had spoken to Jehu: Sons of your line to the fourth generation shall sit upon the throne of Israel; and so it was.

text.NRSV: Shallum son of Jabesh began to reign in the thirty-ninth year of King Uzziah of Judah; he reigned one month in Samaria.
text.Rotherham: Shallum the son of Jabesh, began to reign, in the thirty-ninth year of Uzziah, king of Judah,—for the space of a month in Samaria.
text.KJV: 15:13 ¶ Shallum the sonne of Iabesh began to reigne in the nine and thirtieth yeere of Uzziah king of Iudah, and he reigned a full moneth in Samaria.
text.NABRE: 15:13 Reign of Shallum of Israel. Shallum, son of Jabesh, became king in the thirty-ninth year of Uzziah, king of Judah; he reigned one month in Samaria.

text.NRSV: Then Menahem son of Gadi came up from Tirzah and came to Samaria; he struck down Shallum son of Jabesh in Samaria and killed him, and succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: Then came up Menahem son of Gadi, from Tirzah, and entered Samaria, and smote Shallum son of Jabesh in Samaria,—and slew him, and reigned in his stead.
text.KJV: 15:14 For Menahem the sonne of Gadi, went vp from Tirzah, and came to Samaria, and smote Shallum the sonne of Iabesh, in Samaria, and slew him, and reigned in his stead.
text.NABRE: 15:14 Menahem, son of Gadi, came up from Tirzah to Samaria, and struck down Shallum, son of Jabesh, in Samaria. He killed him and reigned in his place.

text.NRSV: Now the rest of the acts of Shallum, including the conspiracy that he made, are written in the Book of the Annals of the Kings of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Now, the rest of the story of Shallum, and his conspiracy which he made, behold them! written in the book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Israel!
text.KJV: 15:15 And the rest of the actes of Shallum, and the conspiracy which he made, behold, they are written in the booke of the chronicles of the kings of Israel.
text.NABRE: 15:15 As for the rest of the acts of Shallum, with the conspiracy he carried out, these are recorded in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel.

text.NRSV: At that time Menahem sacked Tiphsah, all who were in it and its territory from Tirzah on; because they did not open it to him, he sacked it. He even ripped up all the pregnant women in it.
text.Rotherham: Then, Menahem smote Tiphsah, and all that were therein, and the boundaries thereof, from Tirzah, because they opened not, therefore he smote them,—all the women therein that were with child, he ripped up.
text.KJV: 15:16 ¶ Then Menahem smote Tiphsah, and all that were therein, and the coasts thereof from Tirzah: because they opened not to him, therfore he smote it, and all the women therein that were with child, he ript vp.
text.NABRE: 15:16 At that time, Menahem attacked Tappuah, all its inhabitants, and its whole district as far as Tirzah, because they did not let him in. He attacked them; he even ripped open all their pregnant women.

text.NRSV: In the thirty-ninth year of King Azariah of Judah, Menahem son of Gadi began to reign over Israel; he reigned ten years in Samaria.
text.Rotherham: In the thirty-ninth year of Azariah king of Judah, began Menahem son of Gadi to reign over Israel, ten years [reigned he], in Samaria.
text.KJV: 15:17 In the nine and thirtieth yeere of Azariah king of Iudah, began Menahem the sonne of Gadi to reigne ouer Israel, and reigned tenne yeres in Samaria.
text.NABRE: 15:17 Reign of Menahem of Israel. In the thirty-ninth year of Azariah, king of Judah, Menahem, son of Gadi, became king over Israel for ten years in Samaria.

text.NRSV: He did what was evil in the sight of the Lord; he did not depart all his days from any of the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, which he caused Israel to commit.
text.Rotherham: And he did the thing that was wicked in the eyes of Yahweh,—he turned not away from the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, which he caused, Israel, to commit, all his days.
text.KJV: 15:18 And he did that which was euill in the sight of the Lord: hee departed not all his dayes from the sinnes of Ieroboam the sonne of Nebat, who made Israel to sinne.
text.NABRE: 15:18 He did what was evil in the LORD’s sight as long as he lived, not desisting from the sins that Jeroboam, son of Nebat, had caused Israel to commit.

text.NRSV: King Pul of Assyria came against the land; Menahem gave Pul a thousand talents of silver, so that he might help him confirm his hold on the royal power.
text.Rotherham: Pul the king of Assyria came against the land, so Menahem gave unto Pul, a thousand talents of silver,—that his hands might be with him, to confirm the kingdom, in his hand.
text.KJV: 15:19 And Pul the king of Assyria came against the land: and Menahem gaue Pul a thousand talents of siluer, that his hand might be with him, to confirm the kingdome in his hand.
text.NABRE: 15:19 Pul, king of Assyria, came against the land. But Menahem gave Pul a thousand talents of silver to have his help in holding onto his kingdom.

text.NRSV: Menahem exacted the money from Israel, that is, from all the wealthy, fifty shekels of silver from each one, to give to the king of Assyria. So the king of Assyria turned back, and did not stay there in the land.
text.Rotherham: And Menahem exacted the silver of Israel, of all the mighty men of wealth, to give to the king of Assyria, fifty shekels of silver, of each man,—so the king of Assyria turned back, and stayed not there, in the land.
text.KJV: 15:20 And Menahem exacted the mony of Israel, euen of all the mightie men of wealth, of each man fiftie shekels of siluer, to giue to the king of Assyria: so the king of Assyria turned backe, and stayed not there in the land.
text.NABRE: 15:20 Menahem paid out silver on behalf of Israel, that is, for all the people of substance, by giving the king of Assyria fifty shekels of silver for each one. So the king of Assyria went home and did not stay in the land.

text.NRSV: Now the rest of the acts of Menahem, and all that he did, are they not written in the Book of the Annals of the Kings of Israel?
text.Rotherham: Now, the rest of the story of Menahem, and all that he did, are, they, not written in the book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Israel?
text.KJV: 15:21 ¶ And the rest of the acts of Menahem, and all that he did, are they not written in the booke of the Chronicles of the kings of Israel?
text.NABRE: 15:21 The rest of the acts of Menahem, with all that he did, are recorded in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel.

text.NRSV: Menahem slept with his ancestors; then his son Pekahiah succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: And Menahem slept with his fathers,—and, Pekahiah his son, reigned in his stead.
text.KJV: 15:22 And Menahem slept with his fathers, and Pekahiah his sonne reigned in his stead.
text.NABRE: 15:22 Menahem rested with his ancestors, and his son Pekahiah succeeded him as king.

text.NRSV: In the fiftieth year of King Azariah of Judah, Pekahiah son of Menahem began to reign over Israel in Samaria; he reigned two years.
text.Rotherham: In the fiftieth year of Azariah king of Judah, began Pekahiah son of Menahem to reign over Israel, in Samaria, [and he reigned] two years.
text.KJV: 15:23 ¶ In the fiftieth yere of Azariah king of Iudah, Pekahiah the sonne of Menahem began to reigne ouer Israel in Samaria, and reigned two yeeres.
text.NABRE: 15:23 Reign of Pekahiah of Israel. In the fiftieth year of Azariah, king of Judah, Pekahiah, son of Menahem, became king over Israel in Samaria for two years.

text.NRSV: He did what was evil in the sight of the Lord; he did not depart from the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, which he caused Israel to commit.
text.Rotherham: And he did the thing that was wicked in the eyes of Yahweh,—he turned not away from the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, which he caused, Israel, to commit.
text.KJV: 15:24 And he did that which was euill in the sight of the Lord, hee departed not from the sinnes of Ieroboam the sonne of Nebat, who made Israel to sinne.
text.NABRE: 15:24 He did what was evil in the LORD’s sight, not desisting from the sins that Jeroboam, son of Nebat, had caused Israel to commit.

text.NRSV: Pekah son of Remaliah, his captain, conspired against him with fifty of the Gileadites, and struck him down in Samaria, in the citadel of the palace besides Argob and Arieh; he killed him and succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: And there conspired against him—Pekah son of Remaliah, a hero of his, and smote him in Samaria, in the citadel of the house of a king, with Argob and with Arieh, and, with him, fifty men of the sons of the Gileadites,—and he slew him, and reigned in his stead.
text.KJV: 15:25 But Pekah the sonne of Remaliah, a captaine of his, conspired against him, and smote him in Samaria, in the palace of the kings house, with Argob, and Arieh, and with him fiftie men of the Gileadites: and hee killed him, and reigned in his roume.
text.NABRE: 15:25 His adjutant Pekah, son of Remaliah, conspired against him, and struck him down at Samaria within the palace stronghold; he had with him fifty men from Gilead. He killed him and reigned in his place.

text.NRSV: Now the rest of the acts of Pekahiah, and all that he did, are written in the Book of the Annals of the Kings of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Now, the rest of the story of Pekahiah, and all that he did, behold them! written in the book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Israel!
text.KJV: 15:26 And the rest of the actes of Pekahiah, and all that he did, beholde, they are written in the booke of the chronicles of the kings of Israel.
text.NABRE: 15:26 As for the rest of the acts of Pekahiah, with all that he did, these are recorded in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel.

text.NRSV: In the fifty-second year of King Azariah of Judah, Pekah son of Remaliah began to reign over Israel in Samaria; he reigned twenty years.
text.Rotherham: In the fifty-second year of Azariah king of Judah, began Pekah son of Remaliah to reign over Israel, in Samaria, [and he reigned] twenty years.
text.KJV: 15:27 ¶ In the two and fiftieth yeere of Azariah king of Iudah, Pekah the sonne of Remaliah began to reigne ouer Israel in Samaria, and reigned twentie yeeres.
text.NABRE: 15:27 Reign of Pekah of Israel. In the fifty-second year of Azariah, king of Judah, Pekah, son of Remaliah, became king over Israel in Samaria for twenty years.

text.NRSV: He did what was evil in the sight of the Lord; he did not depart from the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, which he caused Israel to commit.
text.Rotherham: And he did the thing that was wicked in the eyes of Yahweh,—he turned not away from the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, which he caused, Israel, to commit.
text.KJV: 15:28 And he did that which was euill in the sight of the Lord, hee departed not from the sinnes of Ieroboam the sonne of Nebat, who made Israel to sinne.
text.NABRE: 15:28 He did what was evil in the LORD’s sight, not desisting from the sins that Jeroboam, son of Nebat, had caused Israel to commit.

text.NRSV: In the days of King Pekah of Israel, King Tiglath-pileser of Assyria came and captured Ijon, Abel-beth-maacah, Janoah, Kedesh, Hazor, Gilead, and Galilee, all the land of Naphtali; and he carried the people captive to Assyria.
text.Rotherham: In the days of Pekah king of Israel, came Tiglath-pileser, king of Assyria, and took Ijon, and Abel-beth-maachah, and Janoah, and Kedesh, and Hazor, and Gilead, and Galilee, all the land of Naphtali—and carried them away captive to Assyria.
text.KJV: 15:29 In the dayes of Pekah king of Israel, came Tiglath Pileser king of Assyria, and tooke Iion, and Abel-Beth-maachah, and Ianoah, and Kedesh, and Hazor, and Gilead, and Galilee, all the land of Naphtali, and caried them captiue to Assyria.
text.NABRE: 15:29 In the days of Pekah, king of Israel, Tiglath-pileser, king of Assyria, came and took Ijon, Abel-beth-maacah, Janoah, Kedesh, Hazor, Gilead, and Galilee—all the land of Naphtali—deporting the inhabitants to Assyria.

text.NRSV: Then Hoshea son of Elah made a conspiracy against Pekah son of Remaliah, and struck him down, and killed him, and succeeded him, in the twentieth year of Jotham son of Uzziah.
text.Rotherham: And Hoshea son of Elah made a conspiracy against Pekah, son of Remaliah, and smote him, and slew him, and reigned in his stead,—in the twentieth year of Jotham, son of Uzziah.
text.KJV: 15:30 And Hoshea the sonne of Elah, made a conspiracie against Pekah the sonne of Remaliah, and smote him, and slew him, and reigned in his stead, in the twentieth yeere of Iotham the sonne of Uzziah.
text.NABRE: 15:30 Hoshea, son of Elah, carried out a conspiracy against Pekah, son of Remaliah; he struck and killed him, and succeeded him as king in the twentieth year of Jotham, son of Uzziah.

text.NRSV: Now the rest of the acts of Pekah, and all that he did, are written in the Book of the Annals of the Kings of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Now, the rest of the story of Pekah, and all that he did, behold them! written in the book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Israel!
text.KJV: 15:31 And the rest of the actes of Pekah, and all that he did, behold, they are written in the booke of the Chronicles of the kings of Israel.
text.NABRE: 15:31 As for the rest of the acts of Pekah, with all that he did, these are recorded in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel.

text.NRSV: In the second year of King Pekah son of Remaliah of Israel, King Jotham son of Uzziah of Judah began to reign.
text.Rotherham: In the second year of Pekah son of Remaliah king of Israel, began Jotham son of Uzziah king of Judah to reign.
text.KJV: 15:32 ¶ In the second yeere of Pekah the sonne of Remaliah king of Israel, began Iotham the sonne of Uzziah king of Iudah to reigne.
text.NABRE: 15:32 Reign of Jotham of Judah. In the second year of Pekah, son of Remaliah, king of Israel, Jotham, son of Uzziah, king of Judah, became king.

text.NRSV: He was twenty-five years old when he began to reign and he reigned sixteen years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Jerusha daughter of Zadok.
text.Rotherham: Twenty-five years old, was he, when he began to reign, and, sixteen years, reigned he, in Jerusalem,—and, the name of his mother, was Jerusha, daughter of Zadok.
text.KJV: 15:33 Fiue and twentie yeeres olde was he when he began to reigne, and hee reigned sixteene yeeres in Ierusalem: and his mothers name was Ierusha, the daughter of Zadok.
text.NABRE: 15:33 He was twenty-five years old when he became king, and he reigned sixteen years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Jerusha, daughter of Zadok.

text.NRSV: He did what was right in the sight of the Lord, just as his father Uzziah had done.
text.Rotherham: And he did that which was right in the eyes of Yahweh,—according to all that Uzziah his father had done.
text.KJV: 15:34 And he did that which was right in the sight of the Lord: hee did according to all that his father Uzziah had done.
text.NABRE: 15:34 He did what was right in the LORD’s sight, just as his father Uzziah had done,

text.NRSV: Nevertheless the high places were not removed; the people still sacrificed and made offerings on the high places. He built the upper gate of the house of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Only, the high places, took they not away, still were the people offering sacrifice and burning incense in the high places,—he, built the upper gate of the house of Yahweh.
text.KJV: 15:35 ¶ Howbeit, the high places were not remoued: the people sacrificed and burnt incense still in the high places: He built the higher gate of the house of the Lord.
text.NABRE: 15:35 though the high places did not disappear, and the people continued to sacrifice and to burn incense on the high places. It was he who built the Upper Gate of the LORD’s house.

text.NRSV: Now the rest of the acts of Jotham, and all that he did, are they not written in the Book of the Annals of the Kings of Judah?
text.Rotherham: Now, the rest of the story of Jotham, what he did, is, it, not written in the book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Judah?
text.KJV: 15:36 ¶ Now the rest of the actes of Iotham, and all that hee did, are they not written in the booke of the Chronicles of the kings of Iudah?
text.NABRE: 15:36 The rest of the acts of Jotham, with what he did, are recorded in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah.

text.NRSV: In those days the Lord began to send King Rezin of Aram and Pekah son of Remaliah against Judah.
text.Rotherham: In those days, began Yahweh to send against Judah, Rezin, king of Syria,—and Pekah son of Remaliah.
text.KJV: 15:37 (In those dayes the Lord began to send against Iudah, Rezin the king of Syria, and Pekah the sonne of Remaliah)
text.NABRE: 15:37 It was at that time that the LORD began to unleash Rezin, king of Aram, and Pekah, son of Remaliah, against Judah.

text.NRSV: Jotham slept with his ancestors, and was buried with his ancestors in the city of David; his son Ahaz succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: And Jotham slept with his fathers, and was buried with his fathers, in the city of David his father,—and, Ahaz his son, reigned, in his stead.
text.KJV: 15:38 And Iotham slept with his fathers, and was buried with his fathers in the citie of Dauid his father, and Ahaz his sonne reigned in his stead.
text.NABRE: 15:38 Jotham rested with his ancestors; he was buried with his ancestors in the City of David his father, and his son Ahaz succeeded him as king.

text.NRSV: In the seventeenth year of Pekah son of Remaliah, King Ahaz son of Jotham of Judah began to reign.
text.Rotherham: In the seventeenth year of Pekah son of Remaliah, began Ahaz son of Jotham king of Judah to reign.
text.KJV: 16:1 In the seuenteenth yeere of Pekah the sonne of Remaliah, Ahaz the sonne of Iotham King of Iudah began to reigne.
text.NABRE: 16:1 Chapter 16 - Reign of Ahaz of Judah. In the seventeenth year of Pekah, son of Remaliah, Ahaz, son of Jotham, king of Judah, became king.

text.NRSV: Ahaz was twenty years old when he began to reign; he reigned sixteen years in Jerusalem. He did not do what was right in the sight of the Lord his God, as his ancestor David had done,
text.Rotherham: Twenty years old, was Ahaz when he began to reign, and, sixteen years, reigned he in Jerusalem. And he did not that which was right in the eyes of Yahweh his God, like David his father;
text.KJV: 16:2 Twentie yeeres olde was Ahaz when hee began to reigne, and reigned sixteene yeeres in Ierusalem, and did not that which was right in the sight of the Lord his God, like Dauid his father:
text.NABRE: 16:2 Ahaz was twenty years old when he became king, and he reigned sixteen years in Jerusalem. He did not do what was right in the sight of the LORD his God, as David his father had done.

text.NRSV: but he walked in the way of the kings of Israel. He even made his son pass through fire, according to the abominable practices of the nations whom the Lord drove out before the people of Israel.
text.Rotherham: but walked in the way of the kings of Israel,—moreover also, me made, his son, pass through the fire, according to the abominable practices of the nations, whom Yahweh dispossessed from before the sons of Israel;
text.KJV: 16:3 But hee walked in the way of the kings of Israel, yea & made his sonne to passe through the fire, according to the abominations of the heathen, whom the Lord cast out from before the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: 16:3 He walked in the way of the kings of Israel; he even immolated his child by fire, in accordance with the abominable practices of the nations whom the LORD had dispossessed before the Israelites.

text.NRSV: He sacrificed and made offerings on the high places, on the hills, and under every green tree.
text.Rotherham: and he offered sacrifice and burned incense in the high places, and on the hills,—and under every green tree.
text.KJV: 16:4 And hee sacrificed and burnt incense in the high places, and on the hils, and vnder euery greene tree.
text.NABRE: 16:4 Further, he sacrificed and burned incense on the high places, on hills, and under every green tree.

text.NRSV: Then King Rezin of Aram and King Pekah son of Remaliah of Israel came up to wage war on Jerusalem; they besieged Ahaz but could not conquer him.
text.Rotherham: then, came up Rezin king of Syria, and Pekah son of Remaliah king of Israel, unto Jerusalem, to make war,—and they laid siege against Ahaz, but could not overcome [him] .
text.KJV: 16:5 ¶ Then Rezin king of Syria, and Pekah sonne of Remaliah king of Israel, came vp to Ierusalem to warre: and they besieged Ahaz, but could not ouercome him.
text.NABRE: 16:5 Then Rezin, king of Aram, and Pekah, son of Remaliah, king of Israel, came up to Jerusalem to attack it. Although they besieged Ahaz, they were unable to do battle.

text.NRSV: At that time the king of Edom recovered Elath for Edom, and drove the Judeans from Elath; and the Edomites came to Elath, where they live to this day.
text.Rotherham: At that time, Rezin king of Syria recovered Elath, to Syria, and wholly cleared out the Jews from Eloth,—and, the Syrians, entered Elath, and have dwelt there, unto this day.
text.KJV: 16:6 At that time Rezin king of Syria, recouered Elath to Syria, & draue the Iewes from Elath: and the Syrians came to Elath, and dwelt there vnto this day.
text.NABRE: 16:6 (In those days Rezin, king of Aram, recovered Elath for Aram, and drove the Judahites out of it. The Edomites then entered Elath, which they have occupied until the present.)

text.NRSV: Ahaz sent messengers to King Tiglath-pileser of Assyria, saying, “I am your servant and your son. Come up, and rescue me from the hand of the king of Aram and from the hand of the king of Israel, who are attacking me.”
text.Rotherham: So Ahaz sent messengers unto Tiglath-pileser king of Assyria, saying, Thy servant and thy son, I am,—Come up and save me, out of the hand of the king of Syria, and out of the hand of the king of Israel, who are rising up against me.
text.KJV: 16:7 So Ahaz sent messengers to Tiglath Pileser king of Assyria, saying, I am thy seruant, and thy sonne: come vp, and saue me out of the hand of the king of Syria, and out of the hand of the king of Israel, which rise vp against me.
text.NABRE: 16:7 Meanwhile, Ahaz sent messengers to Tiglath-pileser, king of Assyria, with the plea: “I am your servant and your son. Come up and rescue me from the power of the king of Aram and the king of Israel, who are attacking me.”

text.NRSV: Ahaz also took the silver and gold found in the house of the Lord and in the treasuries of the king’s house, and sent a present to the king of Assyria.
text.Rotherham: And Ahaz took the silver and the gold that was found in the house of Yahweh, and in the treasuries of the house of the king,—and sent them to the king of Assyria, as a bribe.
text.KJV: 16:8 And Ahaz tooke the siluer and gold that was found in the house of the Lord, and in the treasures of the kings house, and sent it for a present to the king of Assyria.
text.NABRE: 16:8 Ahaz took the silver and gold that were in the house of the LORD and in the treasuries of the king’s house and sent them as a present to the king of Assyria.

text.NRSV: The king of Assyria listened to him; the king of Assyria went up against Damascus, and took it, carrying its people captive to Kir; then he killed Rezin.
text.Rotherham: So the king of Assyria hearkened unto him, and the king of Assyria came up unto Damascus, and seized it, and carried away the people thereof captive to Kir,—and put Rezin, to death.
text.KJV: 16:9 And the king of Assyria hearkened vnto him: for the king of Assyria went vp against Damascus, and tooke it, and caried the people of it captiue to Kir, and slew Rezin.
text.NABRE: 16:9 The king of Assyria listened to him and moved against Damascus, captured it, deported its inhabitants to Kir, and put Rezin to death.

text.NRSV: When King Ahaz went to Damascus to meet King Tiglath-pileser of Assyria, he saw the altar that was at Damascus. King Ahaz sent to the priest Urijah a model of the altar, and its pattern, exact in all its details.
text.Rotherham: So then King Ahaz went to meet Tiglath-pileser king of Assyria, in Damascus, and saw the altar which was in Damascus,—and King Ahaz, sent unto Urijah the priest, a likeness of the altar, and a model thereof, according to all the workmanship thereof;
text.KJV: 16:10 ¶ And King Ahaz went to Damascus, to meete Tiglath Pileser king of Assyria, and saw an altar that was at Damascus: and king Ahaz sent to Uriiah the Priest the fashion of the altar, and the paterne of it, according to all the workemanship thereof.
text.NABRE: 16:10 King Ahaz went to Damascus to meet Tiglath-pileser, king of Assyria. When he saw the altar in Damascus, King Ahaz sent to Uriah the priest a model of the altar and a detailed design of its construction.

text.NRSV: The priest Urijah built the altar; in accordance with all that King Ahaz had sent from Damascus, just so did the priest Urijah build it, before King Ahaz arrived from Damascus.
text.Rotherham: and Urijah the priest built an altar,—according to all that King Ahaz sent from Damascus, so, did Urijah the priest make it, by the time King Ahaz came from Damascus.
text.KJV: 16:11 And Uriiah the Priest built an altar: according to all that king Ahaz had sent from Damascus, so Uriiah the Priest made it, against king Ahaz came from Damascus.
text.NABRE: 16:11 Uriah the priest built an altar according to the plans which King Ahaz sent him from Damascus, and had it completed by the time King Ahaz returned from Damascus.

text.NRSV: When the king came from Damascus, the king viewed the altar. Then the king drew near to the altar, offered on it his burnt offering,
text.Rotherham: And, when the king came from Damascus, then the king saw the altar,—so the king drew near unto the altar, and caused offerings to ascend thereupon.
text.KJV: 16:12 And when the king was come from Damascus, the King saw the altar: and the King approched to the altar, and offered thereon.
text.NABRE: 16:12 On his arrival from Damascus, the king inspected the altar; the king approached the altar, went up

text.NRSV: and his grain offering, poured his drink offering, and dashed the blood of his offerings of well-being against the altar.
text.Rotherham: And he made perfume with his ascending-sacrifice, and with his meal-offering, and poured out his drink-offering,—and dashed the blood of the peace-offerings which he had, upon the altar.
text.KJV: 16:13 And he burnt his burnt offering, and his meate offering, and powred his drinke offering, and sprinkled the blood of his peace offerings vpon the altar.
text.NABRE: 16:13 and sacrificed his burnt offering and grain offering, pouring out his libation, and sprinkling the blood of his communion offerings on the altar.

text.NRSV: The bronze altar that was before the Lord he removed from the front of the house, from the place between his altar and the house of the Lord, and put it on the north side of his altar.
text.Rotherham: And, the altar of bronze which was before Yahweh, he brought away from the forefront of the house, from between the altar, and the house of Yahweh,—and put it at the side of the altar northward.
text.KJV: 16:14 And hee brought also the brasen altar which was before the Lord, from the forefront of the house, from betweene the altar and the house of the Lord, and put it on the North side of the altar.
text.NABRE: 16:14 The bronze altar that stood before the LORD he brought from the front of the temple—that is, from the space between the new altar and the house of the LORD—and set it on the north side of his altar.

text.NRSV: King Ahaz commanded the priest Urijah, saying, “Upon the great altar offer the morning burnt offering, and the evening grain offering, and the king’s burnt offering, and his grain offering, with the burnt offering of all the people of the land, their grain offering and their drink offering; then dash against it all the blood of the burnt offering, and all the blood of the sacrifice; but the bronze altar shall be for me to inquire by.”
text.Rotherham: And King Ahaz commanded Urijah the priest, saying—Upon the great altar, make thou perfume with the ascending-sacrifice of the morning, and with the meal-offering of the evening, and with the ascending-sacrifice of the king and with his meal-offering, and with the ascending-sacrifice of all the people of the land, and their meal-offering and their drink-offering, and, all the blood of the ascending-offering, and all the blood of the [peace] offering, thereupon, shalt thou dash,—but, the altar of bronze, shall be for me to inquire [into] .
text.KJV: 16:15 And king Ahaz commanded Uriiah the Priest, saying, Upon the great altar, burne the morning burnt offering, and the euening meate offering, and the Kings burnt sacrifice, and his meate offering, with the burnt offering of all the people of the land, and their meate offering, and their drinke offerings, and sprinkle vpon it all the blood of the burnt offering, and all the blood of the sacrifice: and the brasen altar shall be for me to enquire by.
text.NABRE: 16:15 King Ahaz commanded Uriah the priest, “Upon the large altar sacrifice the morning burnt offering and the evening grain offering, the king’s burnt offering and grain offering, and the burnt offering and grain offering of the people of the land. Their libations you must sprinkle on it along with all the blood of burnt offerings and sacrifices. But the old bronze altar shall be mine for consultation.”

text.NRSV: The priest Urijah did everything just as King Ahaz commanded.
text.Rotherham: So Urijah the priest did according to all that King Ahaz commanded.
text.KJV: 16:16 Thus did Uriiah the Priest, according to all that king Ahaz commaunded.
text.NABRE: 16:16 Uriah the priest did just as King Ahaz had commanded.

text.NRSV: Then King Ahaz cut off the frames of the stands, and removed the laver from them; he removed the sea from the bronze oxen that were under it, and put it on a pediment of stone.
text.Rotherham: And King Ahaz cut off the side walls of the stands, and took away from off them the laver, and, the sea, took he down from off the oxen of bronze, which were under it,—and set it on a pavement of stones.
text.KJV: 16:17 ¶ And king Ahaz cut off the borders of the bases, and remooued the lauer from off them, and tooke downe the sea from off the brasen oxen that were vnder it, and put it vpon a pauement of stones:
text.NABRE: 16:17 King Ahaz detached the panels from the stands and removed the basins from them; he also took down the bronze sea from the bronze oxen that supported it, and set it on a stone pavement.

text.NRSV: The covered portal for use on the sabbath that had been built inside the palace, and the outer entrance for the king he removed from the house of the Lord. He did this because of the king of Assyria.
